 263: 
 264: ## File: docs/parse.md
 265: `````markdown
 266: ## Clean one-liner (single object array)
 267: 
 268: ```bash
 269: rg -oP 'href="/title/\Ktt[0-9]+' trailers.html \
 270: | sort -u \
 271: | jq -R . \
 272: | jq -s 'map({imdb_id: .})' \
 273: > trailers.json
 274: ```
 275: 
 276: ---
 277: 
 278: ### What this does
 279: 
 280: 1. Extract only `tt1234567`
 281: 2. `sort -u` → remove duplicates
 282: 3. `jq -R .` → convert each line to JSON string
 283: 4. `jq -s` → wrap into array and map to:
 284: 
 285:    ```json
 286:    { "imdb_id": "ttXXXXXXX" }
 287:    ```
 288: 
 289: ---
 290: 
 291: ## Result
 292: 
 293: ```json
 294: [
 295:   { "imdb_id": "tt36915004" },
 296:   { "imdb_id": "tt6113186" },
 297:   { "imdb_id": "tt32237111" }
 298: ]
 299: ```
 300: 
 301: Saved to:
 302: 
 303: ```bash
 304: trailers.json
 305: ```
 306: `````
 307: 
 308: ## File: docs/sop-setup.md
 309: `````markdown
 310: # NestJS API – End-to-End SOP
 311: 
 312: Scope:
 313: 
 314: - Create NestJS server
 315:     
 316: - Implement `/status` and `/about`
 317:     
 318: - Run locally with **pnpm**
 319:     
 320: - Deploy to **Railway via GitHub `main` branch**
 321:     
 322: - Re-implement cleanly from scratch if needed
 323:     
 324: 
 325: No guesswork. Follow steps in order.
 326: 
 327: ---
 328: 
 329: # 0. Prerequisites
 330: 
 331: Install:
 332: 
 333: - Node.js LTS (≥ 20)
 334:     
 335: - Git
 336:     
 337: - GitHub account
 338:     
 339: - Railway account (GitHub connected)
 340:     
 341: 
 342: Verify:
 343: 
 344: ```bash
 345: node -v
 346: git --version
 347: ```
 348: 
 349: ---
 350: 
 351: # 1. Enable pnpm (via Corepack)
 352: 
 353: Node 20 ships with Corepack.
 354: 
 355: ```bash
 356: corepack enable
 357: pnpm -v
 358: ```
 359: 
 360: If that prints a version → ready.
 361: 
 362: ---
 363: 
 364: # 2. Create NestJS App
 365: 
 366: ```bash
 367: pnpm dlx @nestjs/cli new api
 368: ```
 369: 
 370: Select:
 371: 
 372: ```
 373: pnpm
 374: ```
 375: 
 376: Then:
 377: 
 378: ```bash
 379: cd api
 380: ```
 381: 
 382: ---
 383: 
 384: # 3. Implement Endpoints
 385: 
 386: ## 3.1 Generate controller
 387: 
 388: ```bash
 389: pnpm nest g controller meta
 390: ```
 391: 
 392: ---
 393: 
 394: ## 3.2 Replace `src/meta/meta.controller.ts`
 395: 
 396: ```ts
 397: import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';
 398: 
 399: @Controller()
 400: export class MetaController {
 401: 
 402:   @Get('status')
 403:   status() {
 404:     return {
 405:       ok: true,
 406:       service: 'api',
 407:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
 408:     };
 409:   }
 410: 
 411:   @Get('about')
 412:   about() {
 413:     return {
 414:       name: 'api',
 415:       description: 'NestJS service deployed on Railway',
 416:       version: process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0',
 417:     };
 418:   }
 419: }
 420: ```
 421: 
 422: ---
 423: 
 424: ## 3.3 Register controller
 425: 
 426: Edit `src/app.module.ts`:
 427: 
 428: ```ts
 429: import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
 430: import { MetaController } from './meta/meta.controller';
 431: 
 432: @Module({
 433:   imports: [],
 434:   controllers: [MetaController],
 435:   providers: [],
 436: })
 437: export class AppModule {}
 438: ```
 439: 
 440: ---
 441: 
 442: # 4. Make Server Railway-Compatible
 443: 
 444: Railway injects a `PORT`.  
 445: Never hardcode `3000`.
 446: 
 447: Edit `src/main.ts`:
 448: 
 449: ```ts
 450: import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
 451: import { AppModule } from './app.module';
 452: 
 453: async function bootstrap() {
 454:   const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule);
 455: 
 456:   const port = parseInt(process.env.PORT ?? '3000', 10);
 457: 
 458:   await app.listen(port);
 459: }
 460: bootstrap();
 461: ```
 462: 
 463: ---
 464: 
 465: # 5. Local Development
 466: 
 467: ## 5.1 Install dependencies
 468: 
 469: ```bash
 470: pnpm install
 471: ```
 472: 
 473: If warning about ignored build scripts appears:
 474: 
 475: Ignore unless build fails.
 476: 
 477: ---
 478: 
 479: ## 5.2 Development mode
 480: 
 481: ```bash
 482: pnpm run start:dev
 483: ```
 484: 
 485: Visit:
 486: 
 487: ```
 488: http://localhost:3000/status
 489: http://localhost:3000/about
 490: ```
 491: 
 492: Expected JSON responses.
 493: 
 494: ---
 495: 
 496: ## 5.3 Production build test (important before deploy)
 497: 
 498: ```bash
 499: pnpm run build
 500: pnpm run start:prod
 501: ```
 502: 
 503: Test again:
 504: 
 505: ```bash
 506: curl http://localhost:3000/status
 507: ```
 508: 
 509: If production build works locally → deployment will work.
 510: 
 511: ---
 512: 
 513: # 6. GitHub Setup
 514: 
 515: From project root:
 516: 
 517: ```bash
 518: git init
 519: git add .
 520: git commit -m "Initial NestJS API with status/about"
 521: git branch -M main
 522: git remote add origin <YOUR_GITHUB_REPO_URL>
 523: git push -u origin main
 524: ```
 525: 
 526: Confirm:
 527: 
 528: - `main` branch exists
 529:     
 530: - Code visible in GitHub
 531:     
 532: 
 533: ---
 534: 
 535: # 7. Railway Deployment
 536: 
 537: ## 7.1 Create Project
 538: 
 539: 1. Log into Railway
 540:     
 541: 2. New Project
 542:     
 543: 3. Deploy from GitHub repo
 544:     
 545: 4. Select repo
 546:     
 547: 5. Select branch: `main`
 548:     
 549: 
 550: ---
 551: 
 552: ## 7.2 Set Build & Start Commands (IMPORTANT)
 553: 
 554: Go to Service → Settings → Deploy
 555: 
 556: Set:
 557: 
 558: **Build Command**
 559: 
 560: ```
 561: pnpm install && pnpm run build
 562: ```
 563: 
 564: **Start Command**
 565: 
 566: ```
 567: pnpm run start:prod
 568: ```
 569: 
 570: ---
 571: 
 572: ## 7.3 Confirm PORT Binding
 573: 
 574: Railway auto-sets `PORT`.
 575: 
 576: No need to configure manually.
 577: 
 578: ---
 579: 
 580: # 8. Deploy Flow
 581: 
 582: Deployment happens automatically when:
 583: 
 584: - Push to `main`
 585:     
 586: - Merge PR into `main`
 587:     
 588: 
 589: To test:
 590: 
 591: ```bash
 592: git commit --allow-empty -m "Trigger deploy"
 593: git push
 594: ```
 595: 
 596: Watch Railway logs.
 597: 
 598: ---
 599: 
 600: # 9. Verify Production
 601: 
 602: Railway provides a public domain:
 603: 
 604: ```
 605: https://your-service.up.railway.app
 606: ```
 607: 
 608: Test:
 609: 
 610: ```
 611: /status
 612: /about
 613: ```
 614: 
 615: Must return valid JSON.
 616: 
 617: ---
 618: 
 619: # 10. Full Re-Implementation Checklist
 620: 
 621: If starting fresh or onboarding a new dev:
 622: 
 623: ### Clean Rebuild Procedure
 624: 
 625: 1. Install Node LTS
 626:     
 627: 2. Enable corepack
 628:     
 629: 3. `pnpm dlx @nestjs/cli new api`
 630:     
 631: 4. Implement controller
 632:     
 633: 5. Modify `main.ts`
 634:     
 635: 6. Run `pnpm run build`
 636:     
 637: 7. Test endpoints locally
 638:     
 639: 8. Push to GitHub `main`
 640:     
 641: 9. Connect Railway
 642:     
 643: 10. Set build/start commands
 644:     
 645: 11. Verify deployment URL
 646:     
 647: 
 648: If all steps succeed → re-implementation complete.
 649: 
 650: ---
 651: 
 652: # 11. Common Failures + Fixes
 653: 
 654: ## App not accessible on Railway
 655: 
 656: Cause:  
 657: Hardcoded port.
 658: 
 659: Fix:  
 660: Ensure `process.env.PORT` used in `main.ts`.
 661: 
 662: ---
 663: 
 664: ## “No start command found”
 665: 
 666: Cause:  
 667: Railway not detecting script.
 668: 
 669: Fix:  
 670: Manually set:
 671: 
 672: ```
 673: pnpm run start:prod
 674: ```
 675: 
 676: ---
 677: 
 678: ## Build fails locally
 679: 
 680: Run:
 681: 
 682: ```bash
 683: pnpm approve-builds
 684: pnpm install
 685: ```
 686: 
 687: Only if necessary.
 688: 
 689: ---
 690: 
 691: # 12. Minimal Production Hardening (Recommended Next)
 692: 
 693: After baseline works:
 694: 
 695: - Enable CORS
 696:     
 697: - Add global validation pipe
 698:     
 699: - Add `/healthz`
 700:     
 701: - Add request logging
 702:     
 703: - Add `.env` support via `@nestjs/config`
 704:     
 705: - Add e2e test hitting `/status`
 706:     
 707: 
 708: ---
 709: 
 710: # Final Architecture Summary
 711: 
 712: Structure:
 713: 
 714: ```
 715: src/
 716:   main.ts
 717:   app.module.ts
 718:   meta/
 719:     meta.controller.ts
 720: ```
 721: 
 722: Endpoints:
 723: 
 724: ```
 725: GET /status
 726: GET /about
 727: ```
 728: 
 729: Build:
 730: 
 731: ```
 732: pnpm run build
 733: ```
 734: 
 735: Start:
 736: 
 737: ```
 738: pnpm run start:prod
 739: ```
 740: 
 741: Deploy trigger:
 742: 
 743: ```
 744: Push to main
 745: ```
 746: 
 747: ---
 748: 
 749: ## Add Swagger (NestJS)
 750: 
 751: ### 1) Install packages
 752: 
 753: ```bash
 754: pnpm add @nestjs/swagger swagger-ui-express
 755: ```
 756: 
 757: ### 2) Update `src/main.ts`
 758: 
 759: Add Swagger setup before `listen()`:
 760: 
 761: ```ts
 762: import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
 763: import { AppModule } from './app.module';
 764: import { DocumentBuilder, SwaggerModule } from '@nestjs/swagger';
 765: 
 766: async function bootstrap() {
 767:   const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule);
 768: 
 769:   const config = new DocumentBuilder()
 770:     .setTitle('API')
 771:     .setDescription('NestJS API')
 772:     .setVersion(process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0')
 773:     .build();
 774: 
 775:   const document = SwaggerModule.createDocument(app, config);
 776:   SwaggerModule.setup('docs', app, document);
 777: 
 778:   const port = parseInt(process.env.PORT ?? '3000', 10);
 779:   await app.listen(port);
 780: }
 781: bootstrap();
 782: ```
 783: 
 784: ### 3) Add basic Swagger metadata to endpoints (optional but useful)
 785: 
 786: Edit `src/meta/meta.controller.ts`:
 787: 
 788: ```ts
 789: import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';
 790: import { ApiOkResponse, ApiTags } from '@nestjs/swagger';
 791: 
 792: @ApiTags('meta')
 793: @Controller()
 794: export class MetaController {
 795:   @Get('status')
 796:   @ApiOkResponse({
 797:     schema: {
 798:       example: { ok: true, service: 'api', timestamp: '2026-02-15T00:00:00.000Z' },
 799:     },
 800:   })
 801:   status() {
 802:     return { ok: true, service: 'api', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() };
 803:   }
 804: 
 805:   @Get('about')
 806:   @ApiOkResponse({
 807:     schema: {
 808:       example: { name: 'api', description: 'NestJS service deployed on Railway', version: '0.0.0' },
 809:     },
 810:   })
 811:   about() {
 812:     return {
 813:       name: 'api',
 814:       description: 'NestJS service deployed on Railway',
 815:       version: process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0',
 816:     };
 817:   }
 818: }
 819: ```
 820: 
 821: ### 4) Run + verify
 822: 
 823: ```bash
 824: pnpm run start:dev
 825: ```
 826: 
 827: Open:
 828: 
 829: - `http://localhost:3000/docs` (Swagger UI)
 830:     
 831: - `http://localhost:3000/docs-json` (OpenAPI JSON)
 832:     
 833: 
 834: ### 5) Deploy
 835: 
 836: Commit + push to `main`. Railway redeploys. Swagger will be at:
 837: 
 838: - `https://<railway-domain>/docs`
 839:     
 840: 
 841: If Swagger UI is publicly exposed and that’s not desired, say so—can lock it down by environment or basic auth.
 842: `````
 843: 
 844: ## File: docs/taste-engine.md
 845: `````markdown
 846: # Implement: NestJS “Generate Review Engine” Endpoint
 847: 
 848: ### 1) Endpoint contract
 849: 
 850: **Route (recommended):**
 851: 
 852: - `POST /api/justus/generateReview`
 853:     
 854: 
 855: **Auth / trace / envelope:**
 856: 
 857: - Bearer token guard (same pattern)
 858:     
 859: - Accept `x-trace-id`; always return it
 860:     
 861: - Standard response envelope `{ statusCode, errors, latency, data, traceId }`
 862:     
 863: 
 864: ### 2) Inputs (from generate-review-diagram)
 865: 
 866: Minimum required inputs to match the mobile flow:
 867: 
 868: - `titleId: string` (imdbId)
 869:     
 870: - `spoilerMode: 'spoiler' | 'non-spoiler'`
 871:     
 872: - `forceRefresh?: boolean` (default false)
 873:     
 874: - `provider?: 'openai' | 'deepseek'` (optional)
 875:     
 876: 
 877: These correspond to the diagram path where `generateReviewForTitle(args)` takes `titleId`, `spoilerMode`, and considers `forceRefresh` + provider.
 878: 
 879: **DTO example (shape, not code):**
 880: 
 881: - Validate `titleId` non-empty
 882:     
 883: - Validate spoilerMode enum
 884:     
 885: - Validate forceRefresh boolean
 886:     
 887: - Validate provider enum
 888:     
 889: 
 890: ### 3) Outputs
 891: 
 892: Return the server version of `ReviewResult` (same semantics as mobile):
 893: 
 894: - `decisionType: 'SPEAK' | 'SILENCE' | 'EXPLAIN_CONFLICT' | 'ASK_LIGHT_QUESTION'`
 895:     
 896: - `confidence: number`
 897:     
 898: - One of:
 899:     
 900:     - `reviewText`
 901:         
 902:     - `silenceReason`
 903:         
 904:     - `conflictExplanation`
 905:         
 906:     - `lightQuestion`
 907:         
 908: - `anchorsUsed`, `mismatches`, `structuralMatch` (if present)
 909:     
 910: 
 911: ### 4) Core algorithm (must match the diagram)
 912: 
 913: Implement exactly this flow:
 914: 
 915: 1. **Load reviewGate (per user)**
 916:     
 917:     - Mobile loads from AsyncStorage
 918:         
 919:     - Server needs an equivalent: from DB keyed by user identity (or if user identity doesn’t exist yet, a single global gate config).
 920:         
 921: 2. **Build TasteEvidence**
 922:     
 923:     - The evidence builder pulls title + taste snapshot + gate status + anchors + ratings + contradictions + drift + interactions
 924:         
 925: 3. **Gate check**
 926:     
 927:     - If `evidence.gate.canSpeak === false`:
 928:         
 929:         - Create SILENCE result with `confidence=0` and `reason=gate.reason`
 930:             
 931:         - Persist it (generated_reviews)
 932:             
 933:         - Return it
 934:             
 935: 4. **Cache check**
 936:     
 937:     - If `forceRefresh === false`:
 938:         
 939:         - If cached review exists AND decision != SILENCE, return cached
 940:             
 941: 5. **LLM call**
 942:     
 943:     - Build `system` + `input` (Taste Engine prompt)
 944:         
 945:     - Call LLM Router (next section)
 946:         
 947: 6. **Parse & validate model JSON**
 948:     
 949:     - Must enforce decision enum and required fields (mobile is strict about decision_type validity)
 950:         
 951:     - Strip code fences if present (mobile does)
 952:         
 953: 7. **Persist generated result**
 954:     
 955:     - Save to generated_reviews
 956:         
 957:     - Return ReviewResult
 958:         
 959: 
 960: ---
 961: 
 962: ## 5) LLM Router integration (hard requirement)
 963: 
 964: > POST: `/api/justus/llmRouter`
 965: 
 966: ### Router payload shape (keep it identical)
 967: 
 968: Request to send:
 969: 
 970: - `provider` (optional)
 971:     
 972: - `input` (string, required)
 973:     
 974: - `system` (optional string)
 975:     
 976: - `metadata` (object)
 977:     
 978: 
 979: ### Headers to forward
 980: 
 981: If calling another local endpoint, forward:
 982: 
 983: - `Authorization`
 984:     
 985: - `x-trace-id`
 986:     
 987: 
 988: ## 6) NestJS module structure (per your template)
 989: 
 990: Create feature folder:
 991: 
 992: ```
 993: src/generate-review/
 994:   generate-review.module.ts
 995:   generate-review.controller.ts
 996:   generate-review.service.ts
 997:   dto/
 998:     generate-review.dto.ts
 999: ```
1000: 
1001: Controller rules:
1002: 
1003: - `@Controller('api/justus')` + `@Post('generateReview')` (recommended)
1004:     
1005: - `@UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)`
1006:     
1007: - Don’t use `@Res()` unless streaming/binary
1008:     
1009: - Always return envelope with `statusCode/errors/latency/data`
1010:     
1011: 
1012: ---
1013: 
1014: ## 7) Persistence & idempotency
1015: 
1016: **Tables/collections implied by the diagram:**
1017: 
1018: - `generated_reviews` (store review results) > not applicable to API endpoint
1019:     
1020: - “feed cache” (no server-side cache) > not applicable to API endpoint
1021:     
1022: 
1023: no server-side cache
1024: 
1025: ---
1026: 
1027: ## 8) Error handling (don’t leak internals)
1028: 
1029: Follow the API template rule: never throw raw errors to clients; return `errors[]` in envelope.
1030: 
1031: Recommended mapping:
1032: 
1033: - 400: validation / missing titleId / bad spoilerMode
1034:     
1035: - 404: title not found
1036:     
1037: - 502: llmRouter failed / invalid model output
1038:     
1039: - 500: unexpected
1040:     
1041: 
1042: Always include traceId.
1043: 
1044: ---
1045: 
1046: ## 9) Test checklist (minimum)
1047: 
1048: 1. **Gate false** returns SILENCE and persists it
1049:     
1050: 2. **Gate true** calls llmRouter and parses decision_type strictly
1051:     
1052: 3. **Model returns fenced JSON** still parses (strip fences)
1053:     
1054: 4. **Trace forwarding**: engine ➜ router keeps `x-trace-id`
1055:     
1056: 
1057: ---
1058: 
1059: ## 10) Mobile changes (what gets deleted / replaced)
1060: 
1061: Stop calling `reviewService.generateReviewForTitle(...)` locally and instead:
1062: 
1063: - call `POST /api/justus/generateReview`
1064:     
1065: - use returned ReviewResult to update item state (expandedReview, hasGeneratedReview, etc.)—same state transitions as now.
1066:     
1067: 
1068: Keep the UI behavior (spoiler mode selection, expanded pending state). The engine move is purely backend.
1069: 
1070: ---
1071: 
1072: The following fields need to be passed into ``POST /api/justus/generateReview`` for the taste engine and the AI to generate an accurate review:
1073: 
1074: - title context (at minimum title_id; ideally full metadata: title, year, genre, plot, runtime, etc.)
1075: - spoiler_mode (non-spoiler or spoiler)
1076: - contradictions (unresolved count + examples)
1077: - drift signals
1078: - interactions aggregates (views/skips/saves/likes/listAdds/totalReviewInteractions)
1079: - want_to_watch_rationale (optional but improves explanation quality)
1080: 
1081: So the practical required set is:
1082: 
1083: - user_ratings
1084: - taste_anchors
1085: - taste_snapshot
1086: - gate_state
1087: - title (or title_id + server-side title lookup)
1088: - spoiler_mode
1089: - contradictions
1090: - drift
1091: - interactions
1092: - want_to_watch_rationale (recommended)
1093: 
1094: If your backend can derive some of these from DB using title_id + authenticated user, then mobile can send less. But for accurate generation, the model input should include all of the above evidence fields.
1095: 
1096: ---
1097: 	
1098: ## Postman example: `POST /api/justus/generateReview`
1099: 
1100: ```
1101: 
1102: **Headers**
1103: 
1104: - `Authorization: Bearer API_BEARER_TOKEN`
1105:     
1106: - `Content-Type: application/json`
1107:     
1108: - `x-trace-id: test-review-002`
1109:     
1110: 
1111: **Body (raw JSON)**
1112: 
1113: ```json
1114: {
1115:   "spoiler_mode": "non-spoiler",
1116: 
1117:   "title": {
1118:     "title_id": "tt3118452",
1119:     "title": "The Circle",
1120:     "year": 2017,
1121:     "genres": ["Reality TV", "Competition"],
1122:     "plot": "Contestants live in isolation and communicate through a social media platform to win a cash prize.",
1123:     "runtime_minutes": 97
1124:   },
1125: 
1126:   "gate_state": {
1127:     "can_speak": true,
1128:     "reason": null,
1129:     "updated_at": "2026-02-17T08:00:00.000Z"
1130:   },
1131: 
1132:   "taste_snapshot": {
1133:     "version": "v1",
1134:     "signals": {
1135:       "preferred_tones": ["psychological", "social-strategy"],
1136:       "pace_preference": "medium",
1137:       "violence_tolerance": "low",
1138:       "humor_tolerance": "medium"
1139:     },
1140:     "summary": "Tends to like social-strategy and psychological tension; dislikes mean-spirited humiliation."
1141:   },
1142: 
1143:   "taste_anchors": [
1144:     {
1145:       "anchor_id": "a_social-strategy",
1146:       "label": "social strategy / persuasion games",
1147:       "weight": 0.82,
1148:       "evidence": ["liked: The Circle (concept)", "saved: similar social games"]
1149:     },
1150:     {
1151:       "anchor_id": "a_psych-pressure",
1152:       "label": "psychological pressure in competitions",
1153:       "weight": 0.71,
1154:       "evidence": ["high watch-through on pressure-cooker reality"]
1155:     }
1156:   ],
1157: 
1158:   "user_ratings": [
1159:     {
1160:       "title_id": "tt2267998",
1161:       "rating": 4.5,
1162:       "rated_at": "2025-11-03T10:00:00.000Z"
1163:     },
1164:     {
1165:       "title_id": "tt3118452",
1166:       "rating": 3.5,
1167:       "rated_at": "2026-02-01T10:00:00.000Z"
1168:     }
1169:   ],
1170: 
1171:   "contradictions": {
1172:     "unresolved_count": 1,
1173:     "examples": [
1174:       {
1175:         "type": "tone_mismatch",
1176:         "expected": "clever-social-strategy",
1177:         "observed": "cringe-awkwardness",
1178:         "note": "Sometimes skips second-hand embarrassment content."
1179:       }
1180:     ]
1181:   },
1182: 
1183:   "drift": {
1184:     "trend": "shifting_toward_documentary",
1185:     "confidence": 0.58,
1186:     "signals": [
1187:       { "signal": "recent_likes_more_serious", "weight": 0.6 },
1188:       { "signal": "recent_skips_reality_drama", "weight": 0.4 }
1189:     ]
1190:   },
1191: 
1192:   "interactions": {
1193:     "views": 14,
1194:     "skips": 3,
1195:     "saves": 6,
1196:     "likes": 9,
1197:     "listAdds": 2,
1198:     "totalReviewInteractions": 5
1199:   },
1200: 
1201:   "want_to_watch_rationale": "Interested in persuasion games, but avoiding anything too mean-spirited or humiliation-based.",
1202: 
1203:   "force_refresh": false,
1204:   "provider": "openai"
1205: }
1206: ```
1207: 
1208: ---
1209: 
1210: ## What the API should do with this bundle (non-negotiable validation)
1211: 
1212: If the client is sending taste state, the server must treat it like **untrusted input**:
1213: 
1214: - `spoiler_mode`: enum only (`spoiler` | `non-spoiler`)
1215:     
1216: - `title`: must contain `title_id`; if `title` object missing, allow `title_id` and do server-side lookup
1217:     
1218: - `gate_state.can_speak`: boolean required (or server overrides if it owns gate)
1219:     
1220: - `taste_snapshot.version`: required (so prompt+parsing stays stable)
1221:     
1222: - `taste_anchors`: cap size (ex: max 50)
1223:     
1224: - `user_ratings`: cap size (ex: max 500) and/or require it be **recent window**
1225:     
1226: - `contradictions.unresolved_count`: must equal `examples.length` or be consistent
1227:     
1228: - `interactions.totalReviewInteractions`: compute or verify (don’t trust)
1229:     
1230: 	
1231: ---
1232: 
1233: ## If the goal is “accurate review” without shipping everything
1234: 
1235: Best compromise (usually): client sends **only**:
1236: 
1237: - `title_id`, `spoiler_mode`, `want_to_watch_rationale`, `interactions` aggregates
1238:     
1239: 
1240: Server loads the rest by `userId` from token. That’s smaller, harder to spoof, and keeps taste evolution consistent.
1241: `````
1242: 
1243: ## File: docs/tmpl-api-pattern.md
1244: `````markdown
1245: # TEMPLATE.md
1246: 
1247: ```md
1248: # NestJS API Template (Bearer-secured + Trace-ID + JSON Envelope)
1249: 
1250: ## Contract (all endpoints)
1251: - Path prefix: `/api/...`
1252: - Security: `Bearer <token>` required (verified by `BearerTokenGuard`)
1253: - Trace: every request/response has a UUID `traceId`
1254:   - Incoming accepted via `x-trace-id` (or generated)
1255:   - Always returned in header `x-trace-id` and in JSON body `traceId` (global interceptor)
1256: - Response shape (standard envelope):
1257:   ```json
1258:   {
1259:     "statusCode": 200,
1260:     "errors": [],
1261:     "latency": 123,
1262:     "size": 456,
1263:     "data": {},
1264:     "traceId": "uuid..."
1265:   }
1266: ```
1267: 
1268: ## 0) Prereqs
1269: 
1270: - `.env` must include:
1271:     
1272:     ```
1273:     API_BEARER_TOKEN=replace_me
1274:     ```
1275:     
1276: - App loads `.env` via `@nestjs/config`:
1277:     
1278:     - `ConfigModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true })`
1279:         
1280: 
1281: ## 1) Platform layer (DO ONCE)
1282: 
1283: ### A) Auth Guard
1284: 
1285: File: `src/auth/bearer-token.guard.ts`
1286: 
1287: - Reads `process.env.API_BEARER_TOKEN`
1288:     
1289: - Rejects missing/invalid `Authorization: Bearer ...`
1290:     
1291: 
1292: ### B) Trace-ID
1293: 
1294: Files:
1295: 
1296: - `src/common/trace-id.middleware.ts`
1297:     
1298: - `src/common/trace-id.interceptor.ts`
1299:     
1300: 
1301: Middleware:
1302: 
1303: - If request has `x-trace-id`, validate UUID; else generate UUID
1304:     
1305: - Attach to `req.traceId`
1306:     
1307: - Set response header `x-trace-id`
1308:     
1309: 
1310: Interceptor:
1311: 
1312: - Inject `traceId` into JSON bodies
1313:     
1314: - If handler returns array/primitive, wrap:
1315:     
1316:     ```json
1317:     { "traceId": "...", "data": [...] }
1318:     ```
1319:     
1320: 
1321: ### C) AppModule wiring
1322: 
1323: - Apply middleware for `*`
1324:     
1325: - Register interceptor globally via `APP_INTERCEPTOR`
1326:     
1327: - Import feature modules in `AppModule`
1328:     
1329: 
1330: ### D) Validation (recommended)
1331: 
1332: In `main.ts`, add:
1333: 
1334: - `ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true, transform: true })`
1335:     
1336: 
1337: ## 2) Feature module pattern (FOR EVERY NEW API)
1338: 
1339: Folder layout:
1340: 
1341: ```
1342: src/<feature>/
1343:   <feature>.module.ts
1344:   <feature>.controller.ts
1345:   <feature>.service.ts
1346:   dto/
1347:     <feature>.dto.ts
1348: ```
1349: 
1350: ### Controller rules
1351: 
1352: - Put `@Controller('api/<feature>')`
1353:     
1354: - Put `@UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)` at controller level
1355:     
1356: - Do NOT use `@Res()` unless streaming/binary
1357:     
1358: - Return standard envelope:
1359:     
1360:     - `statusCode`, `errors`, `latency`, `size?`, `data`
1361:         
1362: - Never throw raw errors to clients. Prefer envelope with `errors[]`.
1363:     
1364: 
1365: ### Internal calls
1366: 
1367: If calling another local endpoint:
1368: 
1369: - Forward `Authorization`
1370:     
1371: - Forward `x-trace-id`  
1372:     Use helper: `forwardAuthAndTrace(req)`.
1373:     
1374: 
1375: ## 3) Copy/paste skeleton
1376: 
1377: ### `<feature>.controller.ts` skeleton
1378: 
1379: ```ts
1380: @Controller('api/<feature>')
1381: @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
1382: export class <Feature>Controller {
1383:   constructor(private readonly svc: <Feature>Service) {}
1384: 
1385:   @Get()
1386:   async handler(@Query() q: <Feature>QueryDto): Promise<ApiResponse<any>> {
1387:     const started = Date.now();
1388:     try {
1389:       const data = await this.svc.run(q);
1390:       return { statusCode: 200, errors: [], latency: Date.now() - started, data };
1391:     } catch (e: any) {
1392:       return { statusCode: 500, errors: [String(e?.message ?? e)], latency: Date.now() - started, data: null };
1393:     }
1394:   }
1395: }
1396: ```
1397: 
1398: ## 4) Postman defaults
1399: 
1400: - Authorization: Bearer Token = `{{API_BEARER_TOKEN}}`
1401:     
1402: - Optional header: `x-trace-id: {{$guid}}`
1403:     
1404: 
1405: ```
1406: 
1407: ---
1408: 
1409: # Stub generator layout
1410: 
1411: ## 1) Add these two folders
1412: 
1413: ```
1414: 
1415: scripts/  
1416: gen-api.ts  
1417: templates/  
1418: api-feature/  
1419: feature.module.ts.tpl  
1420: feature.controller.ts.tpl  
1421: feature.service.ts.tpl  
1422: dto/feature.dto.ts.tpl
1423: 
1424: ````
1425: 
1426: ---
1427: 
1428: ## 2) Templates
1429: 
1430: ### `templates/api-feature/feature.module.ts.tpl`
1431: ```ts
1432: import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
1433: import { __FeaturePascal__Controller } from './__featureKebab__.controller';
1434: import { __FeaturePascal__Service } from './__featureKebab__.service';
1435: 
1436: @Module({
1437:   controllers: [__FeaturePascal__Controller],
1438:   providers: [__FeaturePascal__Service],
1439:   exports: [__FeaturePascal__Service],
1440: })
1441: export class __FeaturePascal__Module {}
1442: ````
1443: 
1444: ### `templates/api-feature/feature.controller.ts.tpl`
1445: 
1446: ```ts
1447: import { Controller, Get, Query, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
1448: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
1449: import { __FeaturePascal__Service } from './__featureKebab__.service';
1450: import { __FeaturePascal__QueryDto } from './dto/__featureKebab__.dto';
1451: 
1452: type ApiResponse<T> = {
1453:   statusCode: number;
1454:   errors: string[];
1455:   latency: number;
1456:   size?: number;
1457:   data: T;
1458:   // traceId injected by interceptor
1459: };
1460: 
1461: @Controller('api/__featureKebab__')
1462: @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
1463: export class __FeaturePascal__Controller {
1464:   constructor(private readonly svc: __FeaturePascal__Service) {}
1465: 
1466:   @Get()
1467:   async handler(@Query() q: __FeaturePascal__QueryDto): Promise<ApiResponse<{ ok: true } | null>> {
1468:     const started = Date.now();
1469:     try {
1470:       await this.svc.run(q);
1471:       return {
1472:         statusCode: 200,
1473:         errors: [],
1474:         latency: Date.now() - started,
1475:         data: { ok: true },
1476:       };
1477:     } catch (e: any) {
1478:       return {
1479:         statusCode: 500,
1480:         errors: [String(e?.message ?? e)],
1481:         latency: Date.now() - started,
1482:         data: null,
1483:       };
1484:     }
1485:   }
1486: }
1487: ```
1488: 
1489: ### `templates/api-feature/feature.service.ts.tpl`
1490: 
1491: ```ts
1492: import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
1493: import { __FeaturePascal__QueryDto } from './dto/__featureKebab__.dto';
1494: 
1495: @Injectable()
1496: export class __FeaturePascal__Service {
1497:   async run(_q: __FeaturePascal__QueryDto): Promise<void> {
1498:     // TODO: implement
1499:   }
1500: }
1501: ```
1502: 
1503: ### `templates/api-feature/dto/feature.dto.ts.tpl`
1504: 
1505: ```ts
1506: import { IsOptional, IsString } from 'class-validator';
1507: 
1508: export class __FeaturePascal__QueryDto {
1509:   @IsOptional()
1510:   @IsString()
1511:   q?: string;
1512: }
1513: ```
1514: 
1515: ---
1516: 
1517: ## 3) Generator script
1518: 
1519: ### `scripts/gen-api.ts`
1520: 
1521: ```ts
1522: #!/usr/bin/env ts-node
1523: 
1524: import fs from 'node:fs';
1525: import path from 'node:path';
1526: 
1527: function fail(msg: string): never {
1528:   console.error(msg);
1529:   process.exit(1);
1530: }
1531: 
1532: function toKebab(s: string) {
1533:   return s
1534:     .replace(/([a-z0-9])([A-Z])/g, '$1-$2')
1535:     .replace(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
1536:     .toLowerCase();
1537: }
1538: 
1539: function toPascal(s: string) {
1540:   const clean = s.replace(/[-_]/g, ' ').trim();
1541:   return clean
1542:     .split(/\s+/)
1543:     .filter(Boolean)
1544:     .map((w) => w[0].toUpperCase() + w.slice(1))
1545:     .join('');
1546: }
1547: 
1548: function render(tpl: string, vars: Record<string, string>) {
1549:   let out = tpl;
1550:   for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(vars)) {
1551:     out = out.replaceAll(k, v);
1552:   }
1553:   return out;
1554: }
1555: 
1556: const rawName = process.argv[2];
1557: if (!rawName) fail('Usage: pnpm gen:api <FeatureName>  (e.g. imdb-search or ImdbSearch)');
1558: 
1559: const featureKebab = toKebab(rawName);
1560: const featurePascal = toPascal(rawName);
1561: 
1562: const repoRoot = process.cwd();
1563: const templatesRoot = path.join(repoRoot, 'templates', 'api-feature');
1564: const featureDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'src', featureKebab);
1565: 
1566: if (fs.existsSync(featureDir)) fail(`Feature already exists: ${featureDir}`);
1567: 
1568: fs.mkdirSync(path.join(featureDir, 'dto'), { recursive: true });
1569: 
1570: const vars = {
1571:   '__featureKebab__': featureKebab,
1572:   '__FeaturePascal__': featurePascal,
1573: };
1574: 
1575: const files: Array<{ src: string; dst: string }> = [
1576:   {
1577:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'feature.module.ts.tpl'),
1578:     dst: path.join(featureDir, `${featureKebab}.module.ts`),
1579:   },
1580:   {
1581:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'feature.controller.ts.tpl'),
1582:     dst: path.join(featureDir, `${featureKebab}.controller.ts`),
1583:   },
1584:   {
1585:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'feature.service.ts.tpl'),
1586:     dst: path.join(featureDir, `${featureKebab}.service.ts`),
1587:   },
1588:   {
1589:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'dto', 'feature.dto.ts.tpl'),
1590:     dst: path.join(featureDir, 'dto', `${featureKebab}.dto.ts`),
1591:   },
1592: ];
1593: 
1594: for (const f of files) {
1595:   const tpl = fs.readFileSync(f.src, 'utf8');
1596:   fs.writeFileSync(f.dst, render(tpl, vars), 'utf8');
1597: }
1598: 
1599: console.log(`Created feature: src/${featureKebab}/`);
1600: console.log(`Next: import ${featurePascal}Module in AppModule.`);
1601: ```
1602: 
1603: ---
1604: 
1605: ## 4) Wire it into package scripts
1606: 
1607: In `package.json`:
1608: 
1609: ```json
1610: {
1611:   "scripts": {
1612:     "gen:api": "ts-node scripts/gen-api.ts"
1613:   }
1614: }
1615: ```
1616: 
1617: If `ts-node` isn’t installed:
1618: 
1619: ```bash
1620: pnpm add -D ts-node typescript
1621: ```
1622: 
1623: ---
1624: 
1625: ## 5) How future devs use it
1626: 
1627: ```bash
1628: pnpm gen:api imdb-search
1629: # then add ImdbSearchModule to AppModule imports
1630: ```
1631: 
1632: ---
1633: 
1634: If the current repo is still using “controllers registered in AppModule” instead of modules, keep the generator as-is and just add a “module import step” in the output message (already there). Once the repo migrates fully to feature modules, new APIs become totally mechanical.
1635: `````
1636:

2083:           "mode": "raw",
2084:           "raw": "{\n    \"aiProvider\": \"groq\",\n    \"messages\": [\n    { \"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Hi\" },\n    { \"role\": \"system\", \"content\": \"你好 means hello; it's the standard Chinese greeting you use when meeting someone, including in a restaurant to greet the staff. For more politeness, use 您好; to greet more than one person, say 你们好.\\n\\nExample: Hello, may I see the menu, please?\\nExample: Hello, what do you recommend today?\" },\n    { \"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"give me a sentance.\" }\n    ],\n    \"sourceLang\": \"zh\",\n    \"targetLang\": \"en\",\n    \"context\": \"menu, food\",\n    \"traceId\": \"postman-12345\",\n    \"customPrompt\": \"You are my friend from abroad. You are relatable and kind, sourceLang language tutor. I'm a targetLang speaker. 1. Response must be short, so I can keep up, less than 150 words. 2. Restrict all conversation to sourceLang-targetLang tutoring (address no questions outside this scope). 3. Always provide sourcePronunciation, as Pinyin, for sourceLang words.\"\n}\n",

2086:             "raw": {
2087:               "language": "json"
2088:             }
2089:           }

2273:           "listen": "test",
2274:           "script": {
2275:             "exec": [
2276:               "// Comprehensive tests for POST /api/imageScan endpoint",
2277:               "",
2278:               "// Test 1: Status code validation",
2279:               "pm.test(\"Status code is 200 (Success)\", function () {",
2280:               "    pm.response.to.have.status(200);",
2281:               "});",
2282:               "",
2283:               "// Test 2: Response body exists",
2284:               "pm.test(\"Response body is not empty\", function () {",
2285:               "    pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.not.be.empty;",
2286:               "});",
2287:               "",
2288:               "// Test 3: Response is valid JSON",
2289:               "pm.test(\"Response is valid JSON\", function () {",
2290:               "    pm.response.to.be.json;",
2291:               "});",
2292:               "",
2293:               "// Test 4: Response body structure validation",
2294:               "pm.test(\"Response has expected structure\", function () {",
2295:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2296:               "    pm.expect(jsonData).to.be.an('object');",
2297:               "});",
2298:               "",
2299:               "// Test 5: Check for success indicators",
2300:               "pm.test(\"Response indicates successful processing\", function () {",
2301:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2302:               "    // Check for common success patterns",
2303:               "    const hasSuccessField = jsonData.success === true || jsonData.status === \"success\";",
2304:               "    const hasData = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
2305:               "    pm.expect(hasSuccessField || hasData).to.be.true;",
2306:               "});",
2307:               "",
2308:               "// Test 6: Extracted text validation",
2309:               "pm.test(\"Response contains extracted text data\", function () {",
2310:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2311:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
2312:               "    pm.expect(textField).to.exist;",
2313:               "});",
2314:               "",
2315:               "// Test 7: Extracted text is not empty (if present)",
2316:               "pm.test(\"Extracted text is not empty string\", function () {",
2317:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2318:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
2319:               "    if (textField !== undefined) {",
2320:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.not.equal(\"\");",
2321:               "    }",
2322:               "});",
2323:               "",
2324:               "// Test 8: Response time validation",
2325:               "pm.test(\"Image scan completes in reasonable time (under 10 seconds)\", function () {",
2326:               "    pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(10000);",
2327:               "});",
2328:               "",
2329:               "// Error Handling Tests",
2330:               "",
2331:               "// Test 9: Handle 400 Bad Request errors",
2332:               "if (pm.response.code === 400) {",
2333:               "    pm.test(\"Bad Request (400) includes error message\", function () {",
2334:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2335:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
2336:               "    });",
2337:               "}",
2338:               "",
2339:               "// Test 10: Handle 401 Unauthorized errors",
2340:               "if (pm.response.code === 401) {",
2341:               "    pm.test(\"Unauthorized (401) includes authentication error\", function () {",
2342:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2343:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
2344:               "    });",
2345:               "}",
2346:               "",
2347:               "// Test 11: Handle 413 Payload Too Large errors",
2348:               "if (pm.response.code === 413) {",
2349:               "    pm.test(\"Payload Too Large (413) indicates file size issue\", function () {",
2350:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2351:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
2352:               "    });",
2353:               "}",
2354:               "",
2355:               "// Test 12: Handle 415 Unsupported Media Type errors",
2356:               "if (pm.response.code === 415) {",
2357:               "    pm.test(\"Unsupported Media Type (415) indicates format issue\", function () {",
2358:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2359:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
2360:               "    });",
2361:               "}",
2362:               "",
2363:               "// Test 13: Handle 500 Internal Server Error",
2364:               "if (pm.response.code === 500) {",
2365:               "    pm.test(\"Internal Server Error (500) includes error details\", function () {",
2366:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2367:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
2368:               "    });",
2369:               "}",
2370:               "",
2371:               "// Content Validation Tests",
2372:               "",
2373:               "// Test 14: Check response schema consistency",
2374:               "pm.test(\"Response follows consistent schema\", function () {",
2375:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2376:               "    const hasStandardFields = (",
2377:               "        (jsonData.hasOwnProperty('success') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('status')) &&",
2378:               "        (jsonData.hasOwnProperty('text') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('extractedText') || ",
2379:               "         jsonData.hasOwnProperty('data') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('error'))",
2380:               "    );",
2381:               "    pm.expect(hasStandardFields).to.be.true;",
2382:               "});",
2383:               "",
2384:               "// Test 15: Validate text content type",
2385:               "pm.test(\"Extracted text is string type\", function () {",
2386:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2387:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
2388:               "    if (textField !== undefined && textField !== null) {",
2389:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.be.a('string');",
2390:               "    }",
2391:               "});",
2392:               "",
2393:               "// Test 16: Check for metadata (if applicable)",
2394:               "pm.test(\"Response may include metadata\", function () {",
2395:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2396:               "    // This is a soft check - metadata is optional",
2397:               "    if (jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language) {",
2398:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language).to.exist;",
2399:               "    }",
2400:               "});",
2401:               "",
2402:               "// Test 17: Validate no unexpected null values in success response",
2403:               "if (pm.response.code === 200) {",
2404:               "    pm.test(\"Success response has no critical null values\", function () {",
2405:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2406:               "        const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
2407:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.not.be.null;",
2408:               "    });",
2409:               "}"

2601:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2602:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
2603:               "    if (textField !== undefined && textField !== null) {",
2604:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.be.a('string');",
2605:               "    }",
2606:               "});",
2607:               "",
2608:               "// Test 16: Check for metadata (if applicable)",
2609:               "pm.test(\"Response may include metadata\", function () {",
2610:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2611:               "    // This is a soft check - metadata is optional",
2612:               "    if (jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language) {",
2613:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language).to.exist;",
2614:               "    }",
2615:               "});",
2616:               "",
2617:               "// Test 17: Validate no unexpected null values in success response",
2618:               "if (pm.response.code === 200) {",
2619:               "    pm.test(\"Success response has no critical null values\", function () {",
2620:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
2621:               "        const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
2622:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.not.be.null;",
2623:               "    });",
2624:               "}"
2625:             ],
2626:             "type": "text/javascript",
2627:             "packages": {},
2628:             "requests": {}
2629:           }
2630:         }
2631:       ],
2632:       "request": {

Preserve line breaks. Provide text translation & pronunciation\",\n  \"traceId\": \"postman-base64-uuid-123\"\n}",

2792:           "raw": "{\n  \"prompt_id\": \"{{PROMPT_ID}}\",\n  \"prompt\": \"You are an Educational UI Composer for a mobile app. You OUTPUT STRUCTURED UI BLOCKS that a React Native app can render. Rules: - Output ONLY valid JSON. Use ONLY the block types defined below. - Prefer visual layouts over paragraphs. - Optimize for tutoring and visual symmetry. Minimum 3 blocks per response. - Every block must be self-contained and renderable. - If content is ambiguous, choose the most pedagogically useful representation. Allowed block types: 1. gridVocab 2. examples 3. callout 4. markdown Block schemas: gridVocab: { 'type': 'gridVocab', 'title': string, 'center': { 'text': string }, 'items': [ { 'hanzi': string, 'pinyin': string, 'tone': number, 'gloss': string } ], 'note': string (optional) } examples: { 'type': 'examples', 'title': string, 'items': [ { 'native': string, 'target': string } ] } callout: { 'type': 'callout', 'variant': 'tip' | 'note' | 'warning', 'markdown': string } markdown: { 'type': 'markdown', 'content': string } Tone: A knowledgable friend, speaking as natives would, not from a textbook - Pinyin (space between sylables, in string, for easier reading) - Show examples with Pinyin - Valid JSON EXAMPLE: [ { \\\"type\\\": \\\"gridVocab\\\", \\\"title\\\": \\\"Cool/Cold in Food Context\\\", \\\"center\\\": { \\\"text\\\": \\\"凉\\\" }, \\\"items\\\": [ { \\\"hanzi\\\": \\\"冷饮\\\", \\\"pinyin\\\": \\\"lěng yǐn\\\", \\\"tone\\\": [3, 3], \\\"gloss\\\": \\\"Cold drink\\\" }, { \\\"hanzi\\\": \\\"凉菜\\\", \\\"pinyin\\\": \\\"liáng cài\\\", \\\"tone\\\": [2, 4], \\\"gloss\\\": \\\"Cold dish\\\" }, { \\\"hanzi\\\": \\\"凉面\\\", \\\"pinyin\\\": \\\"liáng miàn\\\", \\\"tone\\\": [2, 4], \\\"gloss\\\": \\\"Cold noodles\\\" } ], \\\"note\\\": \\\"Common food terms using 凉 in Chinese cuisine\\\" } ] - Output text outside JSON\",\n  \"enabled\": true\n}"

4606: ⋮----

4622: export class GenerateReviewDto

5094: `````typescript
5095: import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
5096: ⋮----
5097: type LlmProvider = 'deepseek' | 'openai';
5098: ⋮----
5099: type RouterPayload = {
5100:   provider?: unknown;
5101:   input?: unknown;
5102:   system?: unknown;
5103:   metadata?: unknown;
5104: };
5105: ⋮----
5106: type NormalizedRequest = {
5107:   provider: LlmProvider | null;
5108:   input: string;
5109:   system: string | null;
5110:   metadata: Record<string, unknown> | null;
5111: };
5112: ⋮----
5113: type ProviderResult = {
5114:   provider: LlmProvider;
5115:   model: string;
5116:   trace_id: string;
5117:   output_text: string;
5118:   raw_provider_meta: {
5119:     status: number;
5120:     headers: {
5121:       'x-request-id': string | null;
5122:     };
5123:     body: unknown;
5124:   };
5125: };
5126: ⋮----
5127: type RouteSuccess = {
5128:   statusCode: number;
5129:   body: ProviderResult;
5130: };
5131: ⋮----
5132: type RouteFailure = {
5133:   statusCode: number;
5134:   body: {
5135:     errors: string[];
5136:     trace_id: string;
5137:     details?: unknown;
5138:   };
5139: };
5140: ⋮----
5141: export type LlmRouterResult = RouteSuccess | RouteFailure;
5142: ⋮----
5143: class HttpError extends Error
5144: ⋮----
5145: constructor(
5146:     readonly statusCode: number,
5147:     message: string,
5148:     readonly details: unknown = null,
5149: )
5150: ⋮----
5151: export class LlmRouterService
5152: ⋮----
5153: async route(rawBody: string | undefined, traceId: string): Promise<LlmRouterResult>
5154: ⋮----
5155: // no-op: fallback to OpenAI
5156: ⋮----
5157: private parseJsonBody(rawBody: string | undefined): RouterPayload
5158: ⋮----
5159: private validateRequestPayload(payload: RouterPayload): NormalizedRequest
5160: ⋮----
5161: private async callDeepseek(request: NormalizedRequest, traceId: string): Promise<ProviderResult>
5162: ⋮----

5165: private getDeepseekConfig()
5166: ⋮----
5167: private getOpenAiConfig()
5168: ⋮----
5169: private async fetchWithTimeout(
5170:     url: string,

5173: ): Promise<Response>
5174: ⋮----
5175: private async readJsonSafe(
5176:     response: Response,
5177: ): Promise<
5178: ⋮----

5181:     response: Response,
5182:     body: Record<string, unknown>,
5183: )
5184: ⋮----

5186: ⋮----

5247: function normalizeMimeType(input?: string): string | undefined
5248: ⋮----
5249: async function transcodeToLinear16Wav(inputBytes: Buffer): Promise<Buffer>
5250: ⋮----
5251: /**
5252:  * Accepts:
5253:  * - ["en-US","zh-CN"]
5254:  * - "en-US, zh-CN"
5255:  * - "en-US"
5256:  */
5257: function parseLanguageList(v: unknown): string[]
5258: ⋮----
5259: /**
5260:  * Very strict and predictable validation:
5261:  * - Keeps only BCP-47-ish tags like en-US, zh-CN, es-419.
5262:  * - Removes duplicates (case-insensitive compare).
5263:  * - Hard cap: 1 primary + up to 2 alternatives (max 3 total)
5264:  */
5265: function normalizeLanguages(input: {
5266:   languageCode?: string;
5267:   languageCodes?: unknown;
5268:   altLanguageCodes?: unknown;
5269: }):
5270: ⋮----
5271: // languageCodes takes precedence as the “full set”
5272: ⋮----
5273: // Default if nothing provided
5274: ⋮----
5275: // Validate tag format (simple, deterministic)
5276: const isValid = (tag: string) => /^[a-zA-Z]
5277: ⋮----
5278: // Deduplicate case-insensitively while preserving order
5279: ⋮----
5280: // Hard cap: 3 total languages (1 primary + 2 alternatives)
5281: ⋮----
5282: /**
5283:  * Core runner: returns stable shape.
5284:  * Uses REST Speech API (API key) instead of @google-cloud/speech ADC.
5285:  */
5286: export async function runGoogleSpeechToTextCore(params: {
5287:   body: RouterBody;
5288:   traceId: string;
5289:   audioBase64?: string; // router can pass this from multipart
5290: }): Promise<ProviderOut>
5291: ⋮----
5292: audioBase64?: string; // router can pass this from multipart
5293: ⋮----
5294: // Multilingual selection: primary + alternatives
5295: ⋮----
5296: // Build config + audio per mimeType logic (same intent as old provider)
5297: ⋮----
5298: // Optional model passthrough (same as old provider)
5299: `````

5702: provider?: string | null;   // new

5705: ⋮----

5707: ⋮----

5709: ⋮----
5710: function parseBody(raw: any): TranslationChatBody
5711: ⋮----
5712: // Nest will already parse JSON, but keep parity with the Cloud Function:
5713: ⋮----
5714: function validateBody(payload: TranslationChatBody)
5715: ⋮----
5716: // provider selection precedence:
5717: // 1) provider (new)
5718: // 2) aiProvider (legacy)
5719: ⋮----
5720: function buildAnswer(body: any): string
5721: ⋮----
5722: export class TranslationChatService
5723: ⋮----

5725: ⋮----

5730: // - otherwise 500
5731: // NOTE: If your project uses a global exception filter, this can be simplified.
5732: ⋮----
5733: // Cloud Function used sendError(); without that infra here, throw HttpError
5734: // and let Nest global filters map it.
5735: ⋮----
5736: // Keep log payload similar (no firebase uid here)
5737: ⋮----
5738: private async callProvider(args: {
5739:     aiProvider: ChatProvider;
5740:     messages: any[];
5741:     customPrompt: string;
5742:     traceId: string;
5743: })
5744: ⋮----
5745: // Groq: preserve the same “joined messages” adaptation
5746: ⋮----
5747: // IMPORTANT: if your existing NestJS code already has a groq service,
5748: // swap this section to call it. For now this mirrors the function behavior
5749: // without importing firebase code.
5750: ⋮----
5751: // OpenAI / DeepSeek (same as function)

5895: ## Description
5896: 
5897: [Nest](https://github.com/nestjs/nest) framework TypeScript starter repository.
5898: 
5899: ## Project setup
5900: 
5901: ```bash
5902: $ pnpm install
5903: ```
5904: 
5905: ## Compile and run the project
5906: 
5907: ```bash
5908: # development
5909: $ pnpm run start
5910: 
5911: # watch mode
5912: $ pnpm run start:dev
5913: 
5914: # production mode
5915: $ pnpm run start:prod
5916: ```
5917: 
5918: ## Run tests
5919: 
5920: ```bash
5921: # unit tests
5922: $ pnpm run test
5923: 
5924: # e2e tests
5925: $ pnpm run test:e2e
5926: 
5927: # test coverage
5928: $ pnpm run test:cov
5929: ```
5930: 
5931: ## Deployment
5932: 
5933: When you're ready to deploy your NestJS application to production, there are some key steps you can take to ensure it runs as efficiently as possible. Check out the [deployment documentation](https://docs.nestjs.com/deployment) for more information.
5934: 
5935: If you are looking for a cloud-based platform to deploy your NestJS application, check out [Mau](https://mau.nestjs.com), our official platform for deploying NestJS applications on AWS. Mau makes deployment straightforward and fast, requiring just a few simple steps:
5936: 
5937: ```bash
5938: $ pnpm install -g @nestjs/mau
5939: $ mau deploy
5940: ```
5941: 
5942: With Mau, you can deploy your application in just a few clicks, allowing you to focus on building features rather than managing infrastructure.
5943: 
5944: ## Resources
5945: 
5946: Check out a few resources that may come in handy when working with NestJS:
5947: 
5948: - Visit the [NestJS Documentation](https://docs.nestjs.com) to learn more about the framework.
5949: - For questions and support, please visit our [Discord channel](https://discord.gg/G7Qnnhy).
5950: - To dive deeper and get more hands-on experience, check out our official video [courses](https://courses.nestjs.com/).
5951: - Deploy your application to AWS with the help of [NestJS Mau](https://mau.nestjs.com) in just a few clicks.
5952: - Visualize your application graph and interact with the NestJS application in real-time using [NestJS Devtools](https://devtools.nestjs.com).
5953: - Need help with your project (part-time to full-time)? Check out our official [enterprise support](https://enterprise.nestjs.com).
5954: - To stay in the loop and get updates, follow us on [X](https://x.com/nestframework) and [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/company/nestjs).
5955: - Looking for a job, or have a job to offer? Check out our official [Jobs board](https://jobs.nestjs.com).
5956: 
5957: ## Support
5958: 
5959: Nest is an MIT-licensed open source project. It can grow thanks to the sponsors and support by the amazing backers. If you'd like to join them, please [read more here](https://docs.nestjs.com/support).
5960:

5964: - Website - [https://nestjs.com](https://nestjs.com/)

5972: ## File: tsconfig.build.json
5973: `````json
5974: {
5975:   "extends": "./tsconfig.json",
5976:   "exclude": ["node_modules", "test", "dist", "**/*spec.ts"]
5977: }
5978: `````
5979: 
5980: ## File: tsconfig.json
5981: `````json
5982: {
5983:   "compilerOptions": {
5984:     "module": "nodenext",
5985:     "moduleResolution": "nodenext",
5986:     "resolvePackageJsonExports": true,
5987:     "esModuleInterop": true,
5988:     "isolatedModules": true,
5989:     "declaration": true,
5990:     "removeComments": true,
5991:     "emitDecoratorMetadata": true,
5992:     "experimentalDecorators": true,
5993:     "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
5994:     "target": "ES2023",
5995:     "sourceMap": true,
5996:     "outDir": "./dist",
5997:     "baseUrl": "./",
5998:     "incremental": true,
5999:     "skipLibCheck": true,
6000:     "strictNullChecks": true,
6001:     "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
6002:     "noImplicitAny": false,
6003:     "strictBindCallApply": false,
6004:     "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": false
6005:   }
6006: }
6007: `````
6008:

6080: ## File: src/kiaspora/translationRouter.service.ts
6081: `````typescript
6082: import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
6083: ⋮----
6084: type Provider = 'deepseek' | 'groq' | 'openai';
6085: ⋮----
6086: type NormalizedRequest = {
6087:   provider: Provider | null;
6088:   sourceText: string;
6089:   sourceLang: string; // default "auto"
6090:   targetLang: string;
6091:   context: string | null; // merged context + userMessage
6092:   customPrompt: string | null;
6093: };
6094: ⋮----
6095: sourceLang: string; // default "auto"
6096: ⋮----
6097: context: string | null; // merged context + userMessage
6098: ⋮----
6099: type ProviderResult200 = {
6100:   translation: string;              // canonical JSON string
6101:   detected_source_lang: string;     // best-effort, else "auto"
6102:   provider: Provider;               // who produced it
6103:   latency_ms: number;
6104:   raw_provider_meta: any;           // raw-ish metadata for debugging
6105: };
6106: ⋮----
6107: translation: string;              // canonical JSON string
6108: detected_source_lang: string;     // best-effort, else "auto"
6109: provider: Provider;               // who produced it
6110: ⋮----
6111: raw_provider_meta: any;           // raw-ish metadata for debugging
6112: ⋮----
6113: class HttpError extends Error
6114: ⋮----
6115: constructor(
6116:     public readonly status: number,
6117:     message: string,
6118:     public readonly code?: string,
6119:     public readonly details?: any,
6120: )
6121: ⋮----
6122: function isNonEmptyString(x: any): x is string
6123: ⋮----
6124: function pickFirstEnv(...names: string[])
6125: ⋮----
6126: // Accept both camelCase and snake_case without breaking older clients
6127: function getBodyField(body: any, camel: string, snake: string)
6128: ⋮----
6129: function normalizeProvider(raw: any): Provider | null
6130: ⋮----
6131: function mergeContext(rawContext: any, rawUserMessage: any): string | null
6132: ⋮----
6133: /**
6134:  * Turn model output into a single JSON object string (no fences).
6135:  * - If output contains code fences, strip them.
6136:  * - If it’s parseable JSON, re-stringify compactly.
6137:  * - Otherwise wrap into the required keys (best-effort).
6138:  */
6139: function toCanonicalTranslationJsonString(raw: string, sourceText: string): string
6140: ⋮----
6141: // Try parse JSON as-is
6142: ⋮----
6143: // Keep whatever keys exist, but ensure required keys are present
6144: ⋮----
6145: // fall through
6146: ⋮----
6147: // Best-effort wrapper
6148: ⋮----
6149: export class TranslationRouterService
6150: ⋮----
6151: async handle(body: any, traceId: string): Promise<ProviderResult200>
6152: ⋮----
6153: // Forced provider (no fallback) — matches Cloud Function behavior :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}
6154: ⋮----
6155: // Default: DeepSeek → Groq → OpenAI :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}
6156: ⋮----
6157: // swallow and fall through
6158: ⋮----
6159: // swallow and fall through
6160: ⋮----
6161: private validate(body: any): NormalizedRequest
6162: ⋮----
6163: // NOTE: support both legacy snake_case and newer camelCase
6164: ⋮----
6165: // Preserve contract: merge context + userMessage :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}
6166: ⋮----
6167: // ---------- Providers ----------
6168: ⋮----
6169: private async callOpenAI(
6170:     payload: { sourceText: string; sourceLang: string; targetLang: string; context: string | null; customPrompt: string | null },
6171:     traceId: string,
6172: ): Promise<ProviderResult200>
6173: ⋮----
6174: // IMPORTANT: use the correct env var name
6175: ⋮----
6176: 'OPENAI_KIA_API_KEY',          // ✅ correct
6177: 'OPENAI_API_KEY',              // fallback if used elsewhere
6178: 'OPENAI_KIASPORA_API_KEY',     // legacy safety net
6179: ⋮----
6180: // non-json upstream
6181: ⋮----
6182: private async callDeepSeek(
6183:     payload: { sourceText: string; sourceLang: string; targetLang: string; context: string | null; customPrompt: string | null },
6184:     traceId: string,
6185: ): Promise<ProviderResult200>
6186: ⋮----
6187: private async callGroq(
6188:     payload: { sourceText: string; sourceLang: string; targetLang: string; context: string | null; customPrompt: string | null },
6189:     traceId: string,
6190: ): Promise<ProviderResult200>

6438: ## File: src/justus/trailers.controller.ts
6439: `````typescript
6440: // src/justus/trailers.controller.ts
6441: import { Controller, Get, Query, Req, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
6442: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
6443: import type { Request } from 'express';
6444: ⋮----
6445: type TraceRequest = Request & { traceId?: string };
6446: ⋮----
6447: type TitleDetailBody = {
6448:   requestId: string;
6449:   statusCode: number; // 200 | 206 etc
6450:   dataSource: 'imdb' | string;
6451:   imdbId: string;
6452:   info: any;
6453:   errors: any[];
6454:   traceId?: string;
6455:   timestamp?: string;
6456: };
6457: ⋮----
6458: statusCode: number; // 200 | 206 etc
6459: ⋮----
6460: type ApiResponse<T> = {
6461:   statusCode: number;
6462:   errors: string[];
6463:   latency: number;
6464:   size?: number;
6465:   data: T;
6466: };
6467: ⋮----
6468: function clampInt(n: number, min: number, max: number)
6469: ⋮----
6470: function parseLimit(raw: any)
6471: ⋮----
6472: function sleep(ms: number)
6473: ⋮----
6474: function randomDelayMs()
6475: ⋮----
6476: // 500..1500
6477: ⋮----
6478: function isValidImdbId(s: unknown): s is string
6479: ⋮----
6480: function uniq<T>(arr: T[])
6481: ⋮----
6482: function log(traceId: string | undefined, ...args: any[])
6483: ⋮----
6484: // eslint-disable-next-line no-console
6485: ⋮----
6486: export class TrailersController
6487: ⋮----
6488: async trailers(
6489:     @Query('limit') limitRaw: string | undefined,
6490:     @Req() req: TraceRequest,
6491:   ): Promise<
6492:     ApiResponse<{
6493:       inputFile: string;
6494:       outputFile: string;
6495:       limit: number;
6496:       candidateCount: number;
6497:       requestedCount: number;
6498:       writtenCount: number;
6499:       items: TitleDetailBody[];
6500:     } | null>
6501:   > {
6502:     const started = Date.now();
6503: ⋮----
6504: // 1) Read public/trailer_ids.json
6505: ⋮----
6506: type TrailerIdRow = { imdb_id?: unknown; imdbId?: unknown; id?: unknown } | string;
6507: ⋮----
6508: // ✅ limit enforced here
6509: ⋮----
6510: // 2) Call /api/justus/titleDetail sequentially with 500..1500ms delay
6511: ⋮----
6512: // ✅ CORRECT ENDPOINT
6513: ⋮----
6514: // titleDetail often returns 206; treat 200/206 as success
6515: ⋮----
6516: // 3) Write public/trailers_list.json (atomic)
6517: `````

6536: ⋮----
6537: // 1️⃣ Navigate

6540: ⋮----

6544: ⋮----

6896: ## File: Makefile
6897: `````makefile
6898: ifneq (,$(wildcard .env))
6899: include .env
6900: export
6901: endif
6902: 
6903: PKG_NAME := $(shell npm pkg get name | tr -d '"')
6904: PKG_VERSION := $(shell npm pkg get version | tr -d '"')
6905: RN_VERSION := $(shell npm pkg get dependencies.react-native | tr -d '"')
6906: 
6907: serve:
6908: 	pnpm start:dev
6909: 
6910: bump:
6911: 	pnpm run bump
6912: 
6913: repomix:
6914: 	@mkdir -p temp
6915: 	@npx repomix@latest --style markdown -o temp/repomix-$(PKG_NAME)-$(PKG_VERSION).md
6916: # smaller + more “structural” (less raw noise)
6917: # npx repomix@latest --compress --parsable-style
6918: 
6919: git-push:
6920: 	@npm run bump
6921: 	@git add .
6922: 	@git commit -m "$(m)"
6923: 	@git push origin $(shell git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
6924: 
6925: git-file:
6926: 	@git checkout -- $(m)
6927: 
6928: git-set:
6929: 	git remote set-url origin git@github.com-kiaspora:kiaspora/kia-mobile-app.git;ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_kiaspora
6930: `````

7023: ## File: src/asunder/llmBridge.service.ts
7024: `````typescript
7025: import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
7026: import type { Request } from 'express';
7027: import Busboy from 'busboy';
7028: ⋮----
7029: import { inferMimeFromFilename, parseBase64DataUrl } from './attachment.util';
7030: ⋮----
7031: type ChatMessageRole = 'system' | 'developer' | 'user' | 'assistant' | 'tool';
7032: type ChatMessage = {
7033:   role: ChatMessageRole;
7034:   [key: string]: unknown;
7035: };
7036: ⋮----
7037: type PromptRef = {
7038:   id?: string;
7039:   version?: string;
7040: };
7041: ⋮----
7042: type RequestedAttachment = {
7043:   name: string;
7044:   file_data?: string;
7045:   path?: string;
7046: };
7047: ⋮----
7048: export type UploadedAttachment = {
7049:   filename: string;
7050:   mimeType: string;
7051:   buffer: Buffer;
7052:   size: number;
7053: };
7054: ⋮----
7055: type NormalizedAttachment = {
7056:   filename: string;
7057:   mimeType: string;
7058:   fileData: string;
7059:   size: number;
7060: };
7061: ⋮----
7062: export type LlmBridgeRequest = {
7063:   model?: string;
7064:   messages?: ChatMessage[];
7065:   input?: unknown;
7066:   attachments?: RequestedAttachment[];
7067:   prompt?: PromptRef;
7068:   promptId?: string;
7069:   promptVersion?: number;
7070:   stream?: boolean;
7071:   [key: string]: unknown;
7072: };
7073: ⋮----
7074: export class LlmBridgeError extends Error
7075: ⋮----
7076: constructor(
7077:     readonly statusCode: number,
7078:     message: string,
7079:     readonly body?: unknown,
7080: )
7081: ⋮----
7082: export class LlmBridgeService
7083: ⋮----
7084: async parseMultipart(req: Request, traceId = 'llmBridge-trace'): Promise<
7085: ⋮----
7086: const rejectOnce = (error: LlmBridgeError) =>
7087: ⋮----
7088: async forward(
7089:     requestBody: unknown,
7090:     traceId: string,
7091:     attachments: UploadedAttachment[] = [],
7092: ): Promise<globalThis.Response>
7093: ⋮----
7094: private validateRequest(value: unknown): LlmBridgeRequest
7095: ⋮----
7096: private buildUpstreamPayload(
7097:     body: LlmBridgeRequest,
7098:     defaultModel?: string,
7099:     promptId?: string,
7100:     promptVersion?: string,
7101:     attachments: NormalizedAttachment[] = [],
7102: )
7103: ⋮----
7104: // Backward compatibility:
7105: // Existing callers send Chat Completions-style { model, messages, ... }.
7106: // New upstream is /v1/responses, so translate messages -> input.
7107: ⋮----
7108: // Chat Completions function tools are nested under "function".
7109: // Responses API expects function tool fields at the top level of each tool object.
7110: ⋮----
7111: // If caller omitted model for prompt-based request, allow env default.
7112: ⋮----
7113: // gpt-5 family rejects temperature on the Responses API.
7114: ⋮----
7115: // Responses API uses max_output_tokens, not max_tokens.
7116: ⋮----
7117: // Normalize prompt.version to string for consistency.
7118: ⋮----
7119: private async normalizeAttachments(
7120:     attachments: RequestedAttachment[] | undefined,
7121:     uploadedAttachments: UploadedAttachment[],
7122:     traceId: string,
7123: ): Promise<NormalizedAttachment[]>
7124: ⋮----
7125: private buildResponsesInput(body: LlmBridgeRequest, attachments: NormalizedAttachment[])
7126: ⋮----
7127: private buildMessageContent(
7128:     content: unknown,
7129:     role: ChatMessageRole,
7130:     attachments: NormalizedAttachment[],
7131: )
7132: ⋮----
7133: private normalizeMessageContent(content: unknown)
7134: ⋮----
7135: private buildFileContentItems(attachments: NormalizedAttachment[])
7136: ⋮----
7137: private buildAttachmentInputMessage(attachments: NormalizedAttachment[])
7138: ⋮----
7139: private parseAttachmentDataUrl(fileData: string)
7140: ⋮----
7141: private validateNormalizedAttachment(filename: string, mimeType: string, size: number)
7142: ⋮----
7143: private logNormalizedAttachment(
7144:     traceId: string,
7145:     filename: string,
7146:     mimeType: string,
7147:     size: number,
7148: )
7149: ⋮----
7150: private resolvePromptId(requestPromptId?: string, defaultPromptId?: string)
7151: ⋮----
7152: private resolvePromptVersion(requestPromptVersion?: number)
7153: ⋮----
7154: private invalidRequest(statusCode: number, message: string)
7155: ⋮----
7156: private getUpstreamConfig()
7157: ⋮----
7158: private async fetchWithTimeout(
7159:     url: string,
7160:     options: RequestInit,
7161:     timeoutMs: number,
7162: ): Promise<globalThis.Response>
7163: `````
7164: 
7165: ## File: src/app.module.ts
7166: `````typescript
7167: import { Module, NestModule, MiddlewareConsumer } from '@nestjs/common';
7168: import { APP_INTERCEPTOR } from '@nestjs/core';
7169: import { ConfigModule } from '@nestjs/config';
7170: import { ServeStaticModule } from '@nestjs/serve-static';
7171: import { join } from 'path';
7172: ⋮----
7173: import { MetaController } from './meta/meta.controller';
7174: import { ParseController } from './parse/parse.controller';
7175: import { ImdbSearchController } from './parse/imdb-search.controller';
7176: import { ImdbDetailController } from './parse/imdb-detail.controller';
7177: ⋮----
7178: import { GenerateReviewModule } from './generate-review/generate-review.module';
7179: import { JustUsModule } from './justus/justus.module';
7180: import { AsunderModule } from './asunder/asunder.module';
7181: ⋮----
7182: import { KiasporaModule } from './kiaspora/kiaspora.module';
7183: ⋮----
7184: import { TraceIdMiddleware } from './common/trace-id.middleware';
7185: import { TraceIdInterceptor } from './common/trace-id.interceptor';
7186: ⋮----
7187: ConfigModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true }), // loads .env
7188: ⋮----
7189: export class AppModule implements NestModule
7190: ⋮----
7191: configure(consumer: MiddlewareConsumer)
7192: `````
7193:

``````markdown
  1: This file is a merged representation of the entire codebase, combined into a single document by Repomix.
  2: 
  3: # File Summary
  4: 
  5: ## Purpose
  6: This file contains a packed representation of the entire repository's contents.
  7: It is designed to be easily consumable by AI systems for analysis, code review,
  8: or other automated processes.
  9: 
 10: ## File Format
 11: The content is organized as follows:
 12: 1. This summary section

 14: 3. Directory structure
 15: 4. Repository files (if enabled)
 16: 5. Multiple file entries, each consisting of:
 17:   a. A header with the file path (## File: path/to/file)
 18:   b. The full contents of the file in a code block
 19: 
 20: ## Usage Guidelines
 21: - This file should be treated as read-only. Any changes should be made to the
 22:   original repository files, not this packed version.
 23: - When processing this file, use the file path to distinguish
 24:   between different files in the repository.
 25: - Be aware that this file may contain sensitive information. Handle it with
 26:   the same level of security as you would the original repository.
 27:

 29: - Some files may have been excluded based on .gitignore rules and Repomix's configuration
 30: - Binary files are not included in this packed representation. Please refer to the Repository Structure section for a complete list of file paths, including binary files
