## File: docs/parse.md
``````markdown
 1: ## Clean one-liner (single object array)
 2: 
 3: ```bash
 4: rg -oP 'href="/title/\Ktt[0-9]+' trailers.html \
 5: | sort -u \
 6: | jq -R . \
 7: | jq -s 'map({imdb_id: .})' \
 8: > trailers.json
 9: ```
10: 
11: ---
12: 
13: ### What this does
14: 
15: 1. Extract only `tt1234567`
16: 2. `sort -u` → remove duplicates
17: 3. `jq -R .` → convert each line to JSON string
18: 4. `jq -s` → wrap into array and map to:
19: 
20:    ```json
21:    { "imdb_id": "ttXXXXXXX" }
22:    ```
23: 
24: ---
25: 
26: ## Result
27: 
28: ```json
29: [
30:   { "imdb_id": "tt36915004" },
31:   { "imdb_id": "tt6113186" },
32:   { "imdb_id": "tt32237111" }
33: ]
34: ```
35: 
36: Saved to:
37: 
38: ```bash
39: trailers.json
40: ```
``````

## File: docs/sop-setup.md
``````markdown
  1: # NestJS API – End-to-End SOP
  2: 
  3: Scope:
  4: 
  5: - Create NestJS server
  6:     
  7: - Implement `/status` and `/about`
  8:     
  9: - Run locally with **pnpm**
 10:     
 11: - Deploy to **Railway via GitHub `main` branch**
 12:     
 13: - Re-implement cleanly from scratch if needed
 14:     
 15: 
 16: No guesswork. Follow steps in order.
 17: 
 18: ---
 19: 
 20: # 0. Prerequisites
 21: 
 22: Install:
 23: 
 24: - Node.js LTS (≥ 20)
 25:     
 26: - Git
 27:     
 28: - GitHub account
 29:     
 30: - Railway account (GitHub connected)
 31:     
 32: 
 33: Verify:
 34: 
 35: ```bash
 36: node -v
 37: git --version
 38: ```
 39: 
 40: ---
 41: 
 42: # 1. Enable pnpm (via Corepack)
 43: 
 44: Node 20 ships with Corepack.
 45: 
 46: ```bash
 47: corepack enable
 48: pnpm -v
 49: ```
 50: 
 51: If that prints a version → ready.
 52: 
 53: ---
 54: 
 55: # 2. Create NestJS App
 56: 
 57: ```bash
 58: pnpm dlx @nestjs/cli new api
 59: ```
 60: 
 61: Select:
 62: 
 63: ```
 64: pnpm
 65: ```
 66: 
 67: Then:
 68: 
 69: ```bash
 70: cd api
 71: ```
 72: 
 73: ---
 74: 
 75: # 3. Implement Endpoints
 76: 
 77: ## 3.1 Generate controller
 78: 
 79: ```bash
 80: pnpm nest g controller meta
 81: ```
 82: 
 83: ---
 84: 
 85: ## 3.2 Replace `src/meta/meta.controller.ts`
 86: 
 87: ```ts
 88: import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';
 89: 
 90: @Controller()
 91: export class MetaController {
 92: 
 93:   @Get('status')
 94:   status() {
 95:     return {
 96:       ok: true,
 97:       service: 'api',
 98:       timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
 99:     };
100:   }
101: 
102:   @Get('about')
103:   about() {
104:     return {
105:       name: 'api',
106:       description: 'NestJS service deployed on Railway',
107:       version: process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0',
108:     };
109:   }
110: }
111: ```
112: 
113: ---
114: 
115: ## 3.3 Register controller
116: 
117: Edit `src/app.module.ts`:
118: 
119: ```ts
120: import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
121: import { MetaController } from './meta/meta.controller';
122: 
123: @Module({
124:   imports: [],
125:   controllers: [MetaController],
126:   providers: [],
127: })
128: export class AppModule {}
129: ```
130: 
131: ---
132: 
133: # 4. Make Server Railway-Compatible
134: 
135: Railway injects a `PORT`.  
136: Never hardcode `3000`.
137: 
138: Edit `src/main.ts`:
139: 
140: ```ts
141: import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
142: import { AppModule } from './app.module';
143: 
144: async function bootstrap() {
145:   const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule);
146: 
147:   const port = parseInt(process.env.PORT ?? '3000', 10);
148: 
149:   await app.listen(port);
150: }
151: bootstrap();
152: ```
153: 
154: ---
155: 
156: # 5. Local Development
157: 
158: ## 5.1 Install dependencies
159: 
160: ```bash
161: pnpm install
162: ```
163: 
164: If warning about ignored build scripts appears:
165: 
166: Ignore unless build fails.
167: 
168: ---
169: 
170: ## 5.2 Development mode
171: 
172: ```bash
173: pnpm run start:dev
174: ```
175: 
176: Visit:
177: 
178: ```
179: http://localhost:3000/status
180: http://localhost:3000/about
181: ```
182: 
183: Expected JSON responses.
184: 
185: ---
186: 
187: ## 5.3 Production build test (important before deploy)
188: 
189: ```bash
190: pnpm run build
191: pnpm run start:prod
192: ```
193: 
194: Test again:
195: 
196: ```bash
197: curl http://localhost:3000/status
198: ```
199: 
200: If production build works locally → deployment will work.
201: 
202: ---
203: 
204: # 6. GitHub Setup
205: 
206: From project root:
207: 
208: ```bash
209: git init
210: git add .
211: git commit -m "Initial NestJS API with status/about"
212: git branch -M main
213: git remote add origin <YOUR_GITHUB_REPO_URL>
214: git push -u origin main
215: ```
216: 
217: Confirm:
218: 
219: - `main` branch exists
220:     
221: - Code visible in GitHub
222:     
223: 
224: ---
225: 
226: # 7. Railway Deployment
227: 
228: ## 7.1 Create Project
229: 
230: 1. Log into Railway
231:     
232: 2. New Project
233:     
234: 3. Deploy from GitHub repo
235:     
236: 4. Select repo
237:     
238: 5. Select branch: `main`
239:     
240: 
241: ---
242: 
243: ## 7.2 Set Build & Start Commands (IMPORTANT)
244: 
245: Go to Service → Settings → Deploy
246: 
247: Set:
248: 
249: **Build Command**
250: 
251: ```
252: pnpm install && pnpm run build
253: ```
254: 
255: **Start Command**
256: 
257: ```
258: pnpm run start:prod
259: ```
260: 
261: ---
262: 
263: ## 7.3 Confirm PORT Binding
264: 
265: Railway auto-sets `PORT`.
266: 
267: No need to configure manually.
268: 
269: ---
270: 
271: # 8. Deploy Flow
272: 
273: Deployment happens automatically when:
274: 
275: - Push to `main`
276:     
277: - Merge PR into `main`
278:     
279: 
280: To test:
281: 
282: ```bash
283: git commit --allow-empty -m "Trigger deploy"
284: git push
285: ```
286: 
287: Watch Railway logs.
288: 
289: ---
290: 
291: # 9. Verify Production
292: 
293: Railway provides a public domain:
294: 
295: ```
296: https://your-service.up.railway.app
297: ```
298: 
299: Test:
300: 
301: ```
302: /status
303: /about
304: ```
305: 
306: Must return valid JSON.
307: 
308: ---
309: 
310: # 10. Full Re-Implementation Checklist
311: 
312: If starting fresh or onboarding a new dev:
313: 
314: ### Clean Rebuild Procedure
315: 
316: 1. Install Node LTS
317:     
318: 2. Enable corepack
319:     
320: 3. `pnpm dlx @nestjs/cli new api`
321:     
322: 4. Implement controller
323:     
324: 5. Modify `main.ts`
325:     
326: 6. Run `pnpm run build`
327:     
328: 7. Test endpoints locally
329:     
330: 8. Push to GitHub `main`
331:     
332: 9. Connect Railway
333:     
334: 10. Set build/start commands
335:     
336: 11. Verify deployment URL
337:     
338: 
339: If all steps succeed → re-implementation complete.
340: 
341: ---
342: 
343: # 11. Common Failures + Fixes
344: 
345: ## App not accessible on Railway
346: 
347: Cause:  
348: Hardcoded port.
349: 
350: Fix:  
351: Ensure `process.env.PORT` used in `main.ts`.
352: 
353: ---
354: 
355: ## “No start command found”
356: 
357: Cause:  
358: Railway not detecting script.
359: 
360: Fix:  
361: Manually set:
362: 
363: ```
364: pnpm run start:prod
365: ```
366: 
367: ---
368: 
369: ## Build fails locally
370: 
371: Run:
372: 
373: ```bash
374: pnpm approve-builds
375: pnpm install
376: ```
377: 
378: Only if necessary.
379: 
380: ---
381: 
382: # 12. Minimal Production Hardening (Recommended Next)
383: 
384: After baseline works:
385: 
386: - Enable CORS
387:     
388: - Add global validation pipe
389:     
390: - Add `/healthz`
391:     
392: - Add request logging
393:     
394: - Add `.env` support via `@nestjs/config`
395:     
396: - Add e2e test hitting `/status`
397:     
398: 
399: ---
400: 
401: # Final Architecture Summary
402: 
403: Structure:
404: 
405: ```
406: src/
407:   main.ts
408:   app.module.ts
409:   meta/
410:     meta.controller.ts
411: ```
412: 
413: Endpoints:
414: 
415: ```
416: GET /status
417: GET /about
418: ```
419: 
420: Build:
421: 
422: ```
423: pnpm run build
424: ```
425: 
426: Start:
427: 
428: ```
429: pnpm run start:prod
430: ```
431: 
432: Deploy trigger:
433: 
434: ```
435: Push to main
436: ```
437: 
438: ---
439: 
440: ## Add Swagger (NestJS)
441: 
442: ### 1) Install packages
443: 
444: ```bash
445: pnpm add @nestjs/swagger swagger-ui-express
446: ```
447: 
448: ### 2) Update `src/main.ts`
449: 
450: Add Swagger setup before `listen()`:
451: 
452: ```ts
453: import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
454: import { AppModule } from './app.module';
455: import { DocumentBuilder, SwaggerModule } from '@nestjs/swagger';
456: 
457: async function bootstrap() {
458:   const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule);
459: 
460:   const config = new DocumentBuilder()
461:     .setTitle('API')
462:     .setDescription('NestJS API')
463:     .setVersion(process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0')
464:     .build();
465: 
466:   const document = SwaggerModule.createDocument(app, config);
467:   SwaggerModule.setup('docs', app, document);
468: 
469:   const port = parseInt(process.env.PORT ?? '3000', 10);
470:   await app.listen(port);
471: }
472: bootstrap();
473: ```
474: 
475: ### 3) Add basic Swagger metadata to endpoints (optional but useful)
476: 
477: Edit `src/meta/meta.controller.ts`:
478: 
479: ```ts
480: import { Controller, Get } from '@nestjs/common';
481: import { ApiOkResponse, ApiTags } from '@nestjs/swagger';
482: 
483: @ApiTags('meta')
484: @Controller()
485: export class MetaController {
486:   @Get('status')
487:   @ApiOkResponse({
488:     schema: {
489:       example: { ok: true, service: 'api', timestamp: '2026-02-15T00:00:00.000Z' },
490:     },
491:   })
492:   status() {
493:     return { ok: true, service: 'api', timestamp: new Date().toISOString() };
494:   }
495: 
496:   @Get('about')
497:   @ApiOkResponse({
498:     schema: {
499:       example: { name: 'api', description: 'NestJS service deployed on Railway', version: '0.0.0' },
500:     },
501:   })
502:   about() {
503:     return {
504:       name: 'api',
505:       description: 'NestJS service deployed on Railway',
506:       version: process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0',
507:     };
508:   }
509: }
510: ```
511: 
512: ### 4) Run + verify
513: 
514: ```bash
515: pnpm run start:dev
516: ```
517: 
518: Open:
519: 
520: - `http://localhost:3000/docs` (Swagger UI)
521:     
522: - `http://localhost:3000/docs-json` (OpenAPI JSON)
523:     
524: 
525: ### 5) Deploy
526: 
527: Commit + push to `main`. Railway redeploys. Swagger will be at:
528: 
529: - `https://<railway-domain>/docs`
530:     
531: 
532: If Swagger UI is publicly exposed and that’s not desired, say so—can lock it down by environment or basic auth.
``````

## File: docs/taste-engine.md
``````markdown
  1: # Implement: NestJS “Generate Review Engine” Endpoint
  2: 
  3: ### 1) Endpoint contract
  4: 
  5: **Route (recommended):**
  6: 
  7: - `POST /api/justus/generateReview`
  8:     
  9: 
 10: **Auth / trace / envelope:**
 11: 
 12: - Bearer token guard (same pattern)
 13:     
 14: - Accept `x-trace-id`; always return it
 15:     
 16: - Standard response envelope `{ statusCode, errors, latency, data, traceId }`
 17:     
 18: 
 19: ### 2) Inputs (from generate-review-diagram)
 20: 
 21: Minimum required inputs to match the mobile flow:
 22: 
 23: - `titleId: string` (imdbId)
 24:     
 25: - `spoilerMode: 'spoiler' | 'non-spoiler'`
 26:     
 27: - `forceRefresh?: boolean` (default false)
 28:     
 29: - `provider?: 'openai' | 'deepseek'` (optional)
 30:     
 31: 
 32: These correspond to the diagram path where `generateReviewForTitle(args)` takes `titleId`, `spoilerMode`, and considers `forceRefresh` + provider.
 33: 
 34: **DTO example (shape, not code):**
 35: 
 36: - Validate `titleId` non-empty
 37:     
 38: - Validate spoilerMode enum
 39:     
 40: - Validate forceRefresh boolean
 41:     
 42: - Validate provider enum
 43:     
 44: 
 45: ### 3) Outputs
 46: 
 47: Return the server version of `ReviewResult` (same semantics as mobile):
 48: 
 49: - `decisionType: 'SPEAK' | 'SILENCE' | 'EXPLAIN_CONFLICT' | 'ASK_LIGHT_QUESTION'`
 50:     
 51: - `confidence: number`
 52:     
 53: - One of:
 54:     
 55:     - `reviewText`
 56:         
 57:     - `silenceReason`
 58:         
 59:     - `conflictExplanation`
 60:         
 61:     - `lightQuestion`
 62:         
 63: - `anchorsUsed`, `mismatches`, `structuralMatch` (if present)
 64:     
 65: 
 66: ### 4) Core algorithm (must match the diagram)
 67: 
 68: Implement exactly this flow:
 69: 
 70: 1. **Load reviewGate (per user)**
 71:     
 72:     - Mobile loads from AsyncStorage
 73:         
 74:     - Server needs an equivalent: from DB keyed by user identity (or if user identity doesn’t exist yet, a single global gate config).
 75:         
 76: 2. **Build TasteEvidence**
 77:     
 78:     - The evidence builder pulls title + taste snapshot + gate status + anchors + ratings + contradictions + drift + interactions
 79:         
 80: 3. **Gate check**
 81:     
 82:     - If `evidence.gate.canSpeak === false`:
 83:         
 84:         - Create SILENCE result with `confidence=0` and `reason=gate.reason`
 85:             
 86:         - Persist it (generated_reviews)
 87:             
 88:         - Return it
 89:             
 90: 4. **Cache check**
 91:     
 92:     - If `forceRefresh === false`:
 93:         
 94:         - If cached review exists AND decision != SILENCE, return cached
 95:             
 96: 5. **LLM call**
 97:     
 98:     - Build `system` + `input` (Taste Engine prompt)
 99:         
100:     - Call LLM Router (next section)
101:         
102: 6. **Parse & validate model JSON**
103:     
104:     - Must enforce decision enum and required fields (mobile is strict about decision_type validity)
105:         
106:     - Strip code fences if present (mobile does)
107:         
108: 7. **Persist generated result**
109:     
110:     - Save to generated_reviews
111:         
112:     - Return ReviewResult
113:         
114: 
115: ---
116: 
117: ## 5) LLM Router integration (hard requirement)
118: 
119: > POST: `/api/justus/llmRouter`
120: 
121: ### Router payload shape (keep it identical)
122: 
123: Request to send:
124: 
125: - `provider` (optional)
126:     
127: - `input` (string, required)
128:     
129: - `system` (optional string)
130:     
131: - `metadata` (object)
132:     
133: 
134: ### Headers to forward
135: 
136: If calling another local endpoint, forward:
137: 
138: - `Authorization`
139:     
140: - `x-trace-id`
141:     
142: 
143: ## 6) NestJS module structure (per your template)
144: 
145: Create feature folder:
146: 
147: ```
148: src/generate-review/
149:   generate-review.module.ts
150:   generate-review.controller.ts
151:   generate-review.service.ts
152:   dto/
153:     generate-review.dto.ts
154: ```
155: 
156: Controller rules:
157: 
158: - `@Controller('api/justus')` + `@Post('generateReview')` (recommended)
159:     
160: - `@UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)`
161:     
162: - Don’t use `@Res()` unless streaming/binary
163:     
164: - Always return envelope with `statusCode/errors/latency/data`
165:     
166: 
167: ---
168: 
169: ## 7) Persistence & idempotency
170: 
171: **Tables/collections implied by the diagram:**
172: 
173: - `generated_reviews` (store review results) > not applicable to API endpoint
174:     
175: - “feed cache” (no server-side cache) > not applicable to API endpoint
176:     
177: 
178: no server-side cache
179: 
180: ---
181: 
182: ## 8) Error handling (don’t leak internals)
183: 
184: Follow the API template rule: never throw raw errors to clients; return `errors[]` in envelope.
185: 
186: Recommended mapping:
187: 
188: - 400: validation / missing titleId / bad spoilerMode
189:     
190: - 404: title not found
191:     
192: - 502: llmRouter failed / invalid model output
193:     
194: - 500: unexpected
195:     
196: 
197: Always include traceId.
198: 
199: ---
200: 
201: ## 9) Test checklist (minimum)
202: 
203: 1. **Gate false** returns SILENCE and persists it
204:     
205: 2. **Gate true** calls llmRouter and parses decision_type strictly
206:     
207: 3. **Model returns fenced JSON** still parses (strip fences)
208:     
209: 4. **Trace forwarding**: engine ➜ router keeps `x-trace-id`
210:     
211: 
212: ---
213: 
214: ## 10) Mobile changes (what gets deleted / replaced)
215: 
216: Stop calling `reviewService.generateReviewForTitle(...)` locally and instead:
217: 
218: - call `POST /api/justus/generateReview`
219:     
220: - use returned ReviewResult to update item state (expandedReview, hasGeneratedReview, etc.)—same state transitions as now.
221:     
222: 
223: Keep the UI behavior (spoiler mode selection, expanded pending state). The engine move is purely backend.
224: 
225: ---
226: 
227: The following fields need to be passed into ``POST /api/justus/generateReview`` for the taste engine and the AI to generate an accurate review:
228: 
229: - title context (at minimum title_id; ideally full metadata: title, year, genre, plot, runtime, etc.)
230: - spoiler_mode (non-spoiler or spoiler)
231: - contradictions (unresolved count + examples)
232: - drift signals
233: - interactions aggregates (views/skips/saves/likes/listAdds/totalReviewInteractions)
234: - want_to_watch_rationale (optional but improves explanation quality)
235: 
236: So the practical required set is:
237: 
238: - user_ratings
239: - taste_anchors
240: - taste_snapshot
241: - gate_state
242: - title (or title_id + server-side title lookup)
243: - spoiler_mode
244: - contradictions
245: - drift
246: - interactions
247: - want_to_watch_rationale (recommended)
248: 
249: If your backend can derive some of these from DB using title_id + authenticated user, then mobile can send less. But for accurate generation, the model input should include all of the above evidence fields.
250: 
251: ---
252: 	
253: ## Postman example: `POST /api/justus/generateReview`
254: 
255: ```
256: 
257: **Headers**
258: 
259: - `Authorization: Bearer API_BEARER_TOKEN`
260:     
261: - `Content-Type: application/json`
262:     
263: - `x-trace-id: test-review-002`
264:     
265: 
266: **Body (raw JSON)**
267: 
268: ```json
269: {
270:   "spoiler_mode": "non-spoiler",
271: 
272:   "title": {
273:     "title_id": "tt3118452",
274:     "title": "The Circle",
275:     "year": 2017,
276:     "genres": ["Reality TV", "Competition"],
277:     "plot": "Contestants live in isolation and communicate through a social media platform to win a cash prize.",
278:     "runtime_minutes": 97
279:   },
280: 
281:   "gate_state": {
282:     "can_speak": true,
283:     "reason": null,
284:     "updated_at": "2026-02-17T08:00:00.000Z"
285:   },
286: 
287:   "taste_snapshot": {
288:     "version": "v1",
289:     "signals": {
290:       "preferred_tones": ["psychological", "social-strategy"],
291:       "pace_preference": "medium",
292:       "violence_tolerance": "low",
293:       "humor_tolerance": "medium"
294:     },
295:     "summary": "Tends to like social-strategy and psychological tension; dislikes mean-spirited humiliation."
296:   },
297: 
298:   "taste_anchors": [
299:     {
300:       "anchor_id": "a_social-strategy",
301:       "label": "social strategy / persuasion games",
302:       "weight": 0.82,
303:       "evidence": ["liked: The Circle (concept)", "saved: similar social games"]
304:     },
305:     {
306:       "anchor_id": "a_psych-pressure",
307:       "label": "psychological pressure in competitions",
308:       "weight": 0.71,
309:       "evidence": ["high watch-through on pressure-cooker reality"]
310:     }
311:   ],
312: 
313:   "user_ratings": [
314:     {
315:       "title_id": "tt2267998",
316:       "rating": 4.5,
317:       "rated_at": "2025-11-03T10:00:00.000Z"
318:     },
319:     {
320:       "title_id": "tt3118452",
321:       "rating": 3.5,
322:       "rated_at": "2026-02-01T10:00:00.000Z"
323:     }
324:   ],
325: 
326:   "contradictions": {
327:     "unresolved_count": 1,
328:     "examples": [
329:       {
330:         "type": "tone_mismatch",
331:         "expected": "clever-social-strategy",
332:         "observed": "cringe-awkwardness",
333:         "note": "Sometimes skips second-hand embarrassment content."
334:       }
335:     ]
336:   },
337: 
338:   "drift": {
339:     "trend": "shifting_toward_documentary",
340:     "confidence": 0.58,
341:     "signals": [
342:       { "signal": "recent_likes_more_serious", "weight": 0.6 },
343:       { "signal": "recent_skips_reality_drama", "weight": 0.4 }
344:     ]
345:   },
346: 
347:   "interactions": {
348:     "views": 14,
349:     "skips": 3,
350:     "saves": 6,
351:     "likes": 9,
352:     "listAdds": 2,
353:     "totalReviewInteractions": 5
354:   },
355: 
356:   "want_to_watch_rationale": "Interested in persuasion games, but avoiding anything too mean-spirited or humiliation-based.",
357: 
358:   "force_refresh": false,
359:   "provider": "openai"
360: }
361: ```
362: 
363: ---
364: 
365: ## What the API should do with this bundle (non-negotiable validation)
366: 
367: If the client is sending taste state, the server must treat it like **untrusted input**:
368: 
369: - `spoiler_mode`: enum only (`spoiler` | `non-spoiler`)
370:     
371: - `title`: must contain `title_id`; if `title` object missing, allow `title_id` and do server-side lookup
372:     
373: - `gate_state.can_speak`: boolean required (or server overrides if it owns gate)
374:     
375: - `taste_snapshot.version`: required (so prompt+parsing stays stable)
376:     
377: - `taste_anchors`: cap size (ex: max 50)
378:     
379: - `user_ratings`: cap size (ex: max 500) and/or require it be **recent window**
380:     
381: - `contradictions.unresolved_count`: must equal `examples.length` or be consistent
382:     
383: - `interactions.totalReviewInteractions`: compute or verify (don’t trust)
384:     
385: 	
386: ---
387: 
388: ## If the goal is “accurate review” without shipping everything
389: 
390: Best compromise (usually): client sends **only**:
391: 
392: - `title_id`, `spoiler_mode`, `want_to_watch_rationale`, `interactions` aggregates
393:     
394: 
395: Server loads the rest by `userId` from token. That’s smaller, harder to spoof, and keeps taste evolution consistent.
``````

## File: docs/tmpl-api-pattern.md
``````markdown
  1: # TEMPLATE.md
  2: 
  3: ```md
  4: # NestJS API Template (Bearer-secured + Trace-ID + JSON Envelope)
  5: 
  6: ## Contract (all endpoints)
  7: - Path prefix: `/api/...`
  8: - Security: `Bearer <token>` required (verified by `BearerTokenGuard`)
  9: - Trace: every request/response has a UUID `traceId`
 10:   - Incoming accepted via `x-trace-id` (or generated)
 11:   - Always returned in header `x-trace-id` and in JSON body `traceId` (global interceptor)
 12: - Response shape (standard envelope):
 13:   ```json
 14:   {
 15:     "statusCode": 200,
 16:     "errors": [],
 17:     "latency": 123,
 18:     "size": 456,
 19:     "data": {},
 20:     "traceId": "uuid..."
 21:   }
 22: ```
 23: 
 24: ## 0) Prereqs
 25: 
 26: - `.env` must include:
 27:     
 28:     ```
 29:     API_BEARER_TOKEN=replace_me
 30:     ```
 31:     
 32: - App loads `.env` via `@nestjs/config`:
 33:     
 34:     - `ConfigModule.forRoot({ isGlobal: true })`
 35:         
 36: 
 37: ## 1) Platform layer (DO ONCE)
 38: 
 39: ### A) Auth Guard
 40: 
 41: File: `src/auth/bearer-token.guard.ts`
 42: 
 43: - Reads `process.env.API_BEARER_TOKEN`
 44:     
 45: - Rejects missing/invalid `Authorization: Bearer ...`
 46:     
 47: 
 48: ### B) Trace-ID
 49: 
 50: Files:
 51: 
 52: - `src/common/trace-id.middleware.ts`
 53:     
 54: - `src/common/trace-id.interceptor.ts`
 55:     
 56: 
 57: Middleware:
 58: 
 59: - If request has `x-trace-id`, validate UUID; else generate UUID
 60:     
 61: - Attach to `req.traceId`
 62:     
 63: - Set response header `x-trace-id`
 64:     
 65: 
 66: Interceptor:
 67: 
 68: - Inject `traceId` into JSON bodies
 69:     
 70: - If handler returns array/primitive, wrap:
 71:     
 72:     ```json
 73:     { "traceId": "...", "data": [...] }
 74:     ```
 75:     
 76: 
 77: ### C) AppModule wiring
 78: 
 79: - Apply middleware for `*`
 80:     
 81: - Register interceptor globally via `APP_INTERCEPTOR`
 82:     
 83: - Import feature modules in `AppModule`
 84:     
 85: 
 86: ### D) Validation (recommended)
 87: 
 88: In `main.ts`, add:
 89: 
 90: - `ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true, transform: true })`
 91:     
 92: 
 93: ## 2) Feature module pattern (FOR EVERY NEW API)
 94: 
 95: Folder layout:
 96: 
 97: ```
 98: src/<feature>/
 99:   <feature>.module.ts
100:   <feature>.controller.ts
101:   <feature>.service.ts
102:   dto/
103:     <feature>.dto.ts
104: ```
105: 
106: ### Controller rules
107: 
108: - Put `@Controller('api/<feature>')`
109:     
110: - Put `@UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)` at controller level
111:     
112: - Do NOT use `@Res()` unless streaming/binary
113:     
114: - Return standard envelope:
115:     
116:     - `statusCode`, `errors`, `latency`, `size?`, `data`
117:         
118: - Never throw raw errors to clients. Prefer envelope with `errors[]`.
119:     
120: 
121: ### Internal calls
122: 
123: If calling another local endpoint:
124: 
125: - Forward `Authorization`
126:     
127: - Forward `x-trace-id`  
128:     Use helper: `forwardAuthAndTrace(req)`.
129:     
130: 
131: ## 3) Copy/paste skeleton
132: 
133: ### `<feature>.controller.ts` skeleton
134: 
135: ```ts
136: @Controller('api/<feature>')
137: @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
138: export class <Feature>Controller {
139:   constructor(private readonly svc: <Feature>Service) {}
140: 
141:   @Get()
142:   async handler(@Query() q: <Feature>QueryDto): Promise<ApiResponse<any>> {
143:     const started = Date.now();
144:     try {
145:       const data = await this.svc.run(q);
146:       return { statusCode: 200, errors: [], latency: Date.now() - started, data };
147:     } catch (e: any) {
148:       return { statusCode: 500, errors: [String(e?.message ?? e)], latency: Date.now() - started, data: null };
149:     }
150:   }
151: }
152: ```
153: 
154: ## 4) Postman defaults
155: 
156: - Authorization: Bearer Token = `{{API_BEARER_TOKEN}}`
157:     
158: - Optional header: `x-trace-id: {{$guid}}`
159:     
160: 
161: ```
162: 
163: ---
164: 
165: # Stub generator layout
166: 
167: ## 1) Add these two folders
168: 
169: ```
170: 
171: scripts/  
172: gen-api.ts  
173: templates/  
174: api-feature/  
175: feature.module.ts.tpl  
176: feature.controller.ts.tpl  
177: feature.service.ts.tpl  
178: dto/feature.dto.ts.tpl
179: 
180: ````
181: 
182: ---
183: 
184: ## 2) Templates
185: 
186: ### `templates/api-feature/feature.module.ts.tpl`
187: ```ts
188: import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
189: import { __FeaturePascal__Controller } from './__featureKebab__.controller';
190: import { __FeaturePascal__Service } from './__featureKebab__.service';
191: 
192: @Module({
193:   controllers: [__FeaturePascal__Controller],
194:   providers: [__FeaturePascal__Service],
195:   exports: [__FeaturePascal__Service],
196: })
197: export class __FeaturePascal__Module {}
198: ````
199: 
200: ### `templates/api-feature/feature.controller.ts.tpl`
201: 
202: ```ts
203: import { Controller, Get, Query, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
204: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
205: import { __FeaturePascal__Service } from './__featureKebab__.service';
206: import { __FeaturePascal__QueryDto } from './dto/__featureKebab__.dto';
207: 
208: type ApiResponse<T> = {
209:   statusCode: number;
210:   errors: string[];
211:   latency: number;
212:   size?: number;
213:   data: T;
214:   // traceId injected by interceptor
215: };
216: 
217: @Controller('api/__featureKebab__')
218: @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
219: export class __FeaturePascal__Controller {
220:   constructor(private readonly svc: __FeaturePascal__Service) {}
221: 
222:   @Get()
223:   async handler(@Query() q: __FeaturePascal__QueryDto): Promise<ApiResponse<{ ok: true } | null>> {
224:     const started = Date.now();
225:     try {
226:       await this.svc.run(q);
227:       return {
228:         statusCode: 200,
229:         errors: [],
230:         latency: Date.now() - started,
231:         data: { ok: true },
232:       };
233:     } catch (e: any) {
234:       return {
235:         statusCode: 500,
236:         errors: [String(e?.message ?? e)],
237:         latency: Date.now() - started,
238:         data: null,
239:       };
240:     }
241:   }
242: }
243: ```
244: 
245: ### `templates/api-feature/feature.service.ts.tpl`
246: 
247: ```ts
248: import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
249: import { __FeaturePascal__QueryDto } from './dto/__featureKebab__.dto';
250: 
251: @Injectable()
252: export class __FeaturePascal__Service {
253:   async run(_q: __FeaturePascal__QueryDto): Promise<void> {
254:     // TODO: implement
255:   }
256: }
257: ```
258: 
259: ### `templates/api-feature/dto/feature.dto.ts.tpl`
260: 
261: ```ts
262: import { IsOptional, IsString } from 'class-validator';
263: 
264: export class __FeaturePascal__QueryDto {
265:   @IsOptional()
266:   @IsString()
267:   q?: string;
268: }
269: ```
270: 
271: ---
272: 
273: ## 3) Generator script
274: 
275: ### `scripts/gen-api.ts`
276: 
277: ```ts
278: #!/usr/bin/env ts-node
279: 
280: import fs from 'node:fs';
281: import path from 'node:path';
282: 
283: function fail(msg: string): never {
284:   console.error(msg);
285:   process.exit(1);
286: }
287: 
288: function toKebab(s: string) {
289:   return s
290:     .replace(/([a-z0-9])([A-Z])/g, '$1-$2')
291:     .replace(/[\s_]+/g, '-')
292:     .toLowerCase();
293: }
294: 
295: function toPascal(s: string) {
296:   const clean = s.replace(/[-_]/g, ' ').trim();
297:   return clean
298:     .split(/\s+/)
299:     .filter(Boolean)
300:     .map((w) => w[0].toUpperCase() + w.slice(1))
301:     .join('');
302: }
303: 
304: function render(tpl: string, vars: Record<string, string>) {
305:   let out = tpl;
306:   for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(vars)) {
307:     out = out.replaceAll(k, v);
308:   }
309:   return out;
310: }
311: 
312: const rawName = process.argv[2];
313: if (!rawName) fail('Usage: pnpm gen:api <FeatureName>  (e.g. imdb-search or ImdbSearch)');
314: 
315: const featureKebab = toKebab(rawName);
316: const featurePascal = toPascal(rawName);
317: 
318: const repoRoot = process.cwd();
319: const templatesRoot = path.join(repoRoot, 'templates', 'api-feature');
320: const featureDir = path.join(repoRoot, 'src', featureKebab);
321: 
322: if (fs.existsSync(featureDir)) fail(`Feature already exists: ${featureDir}`);
323: 
324: fs.mkdirSync(path.join(featureDir, 'dto'), { recursive: true });
325: 
326: const vars = {
327:   '__featureKebab__': featureKebab,
328:   '__FeaturePascal__': featurePascal,
329: };
330: 
331: const files: Array<{ src: string; dst: string }> = [
332:   {
333:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'feature.module.ts.tpl'),
334:     dst: path.join(featureDir, `${featureKebab}.module.ts`),
335:   },
336:   {
337:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'feature.controller.ts.tpl'),
338:     dst: path.join(featureDir, `${featureKebab}.controller.ts`),
339:   },
340:   {
341:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'feature.service.ts.tpl'),
342:     dst: path.join(featureDir, `${featureKebab}.service.ts`),
343:   },
344:   {
345:     src: path.join(templatesRoot, 'dto', 'feature.dto.ts.tpl'),
346:     dst: path.join(featureDir, 'dto', `${featureKebab}.dto.ts`),
347:   },
348: ];
349: 
350: for (const f of files) {
351:   const tpl = fs.readFileSync(f.src, 'utf8');
352:   fs.writeFileSync(f.dst, render(tpl, vars), 'utf8');
353: }
354: 
355: console.log(`Created feature: src/${featureKebab}/`);
356: console.log(`Next: import ${featurePascal}Module in AppModule.`);
357: ```
358: 
359: ---
360: 
361: ## 4) Wire it into package scripts
362: 
363: In `package.json`:
364: 
365: ```json
366: {
367:   "scripts": {
368:     "gen:api": "ts-node scripts/gen-api.ts"
369:   }
370: }
371: ```
372: 
373: If `ts-node` isn’t installed:
374: 
375: ```bash
376: pnpm add -D ts-node typescript
377: ```
378: 
379: ---
380: 
381: ## 5) How future devs use it
382: 
383: ```bash
384: pnpm gen:api imdb-search
385: # then add ImdbSearchModule to AppModule imports
386: ```
387: 
388: ---
389: 
390: If the current repo is still using “controllers registered in AppModule” instead of modules, keep the generator as-is and just add a “module import step” in the output message (already there). Once the repo migrates fully to feature modules, new APIs become totally mechanical.
``````

  93:               "pm.test(\"Response body is not empty\", function () {",

  95:               "});",
  96:               "",

  99:               "    pm.response.to.be.json;",

 101:               "",

 106:               "});",
 107:               "",
 108:               "// Test 5: Check for success indicators",
 109:               "pm.test(\"Response indicates successful processing\", function () {",
 110:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",

 112:               "    const hasSuccessField = jsonData.success === true || jsonData.status === \"success\";",
 113:               "    const hasData = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 114:               "    pm.expect(hasSuccessField || hasData).to.be.true;",
 115:               "});",
 116:               "",

 118:               "pm.test(\"Response contains extracted text data\", function () {",
 119:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 120:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 121:               "    pm.expect(textField).to.exist;",
 122:               "});",
 123:               "",
 124:               "// Test 7: Extracted text is not empty (if present)",
 125:               "pm.test(\"Extracted text is not empty string\", function () {",
 126:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 127:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 128:               "    if (textField !== undefined) {",
 129:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.not.equal(\"\");",
 130:               "    }",
 131:               "});",
 132:               "",
 133:               "// Test 8: Response time validation",
 134:               "pm.test(\"Image scan completes in reasonable time (under 10 seconds)\", function () {",
 135:               "    pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(10000);",
 136:               "});",
 137:               "",
 138:               "// Error Handling Tests",
 139:               "",
 140:               "// Test 9: Handle 400 Bad Request errors",
 141:               "if (pm.response.code === 400) {",
 142:               "    pm.test(\"Bad Request (400) includes error message\", function () {",
 143:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 144:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 145:               "    });",
 146:               "}",
 147:               "",
 148:               "// Test 10: Handle 401 Unauthorized errors",
 149:               "if (pm.response.code === 401) {",
 150:               "    pm.test(\"Unauthorized (401) includes authentication error\", function () {",
 151:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 152:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 153:               "    });",
 154:               "}",
 155:               "",
 156:               "// Test 11: Handle 413 Payload Too Large errors",
 157:               "if (pm.response.code === 413) {",
 158:               "    pm.test(\"Payload Too Large (413) indicates file size issue\", function () {",
 159:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 160:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 161:               "    });",
 162:               "}",
 163:               "",
 164:               "// Test 12: Handle 415 Unsupported Media Type errors",
 165:               "if (pm.response.code === 415) {",
 166:               "    pm.test(\"Unsupported Media Type (415) indicates format issue\", function () {",
 167:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 168:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 169:               "    });",
 170:               "}",
 171:               "",
 172:               "// Test 13: Handle 500 Internal Server Error",
 173:               "if (pm.response.code === 500) {",
 174:               "    pm.test(\"Internal Server Error (500) includes error details\", function () {",
 175:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 176:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 177:               "    });",
 178:               "}",
 179:               "",
 180:               "// Content Validation Tests",
 181:               "",
 182:               "// Test 14: Check response schema consistency",
 183:               "pm.test(\"Response follows consistent schema\", function () {",
 184:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 185:               "    const hasStandardFields = (",
 186:               "        (jsonData.hasOwnProperty('success') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('status')) &&",
 187:               "        (jsonData.hasOwnProperty('text') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('extractedText') || ",
 188:               "         jsonData.hasOwnProperty('data') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('error'))",
 189:               "    );",
 190:               "    pm.expect(hasStandardFields).to.be.true;",
 191:               "});",
 192:               "",
 193:               "// Test 15: Validate text content type",
 194:               "pm.test(\"Extracted text is string type\", function () {",
 195:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 196:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 197:               "    if (textField !== undefined && textField !== null) {",
 198:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.be.a('string');",
 199:               "    }",
 200:               "});",
 201:               "",
 202:               "// Test 16: Check for metadata (if applicable)",
 203:               "pm.test(\"Response may include metadata\", function () {",
 204:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 205:               "    // This is a soft check - metadata is optional",
 206:               "    if (jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language) {",
 207:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language).to.exist;",
 208:               "    }",
 209:               "});",
 210:               "",
 211:               "// Test 17: Validate no unexpected null values in success response",
 212:               "if (pm.response.code === 200) {",
 213:               "    pm.test(\"Success response has no critical null values\", function () {",

 215:               "        const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 216:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.not.be.null;",
 217:               "    });",
 218:               "}"

 381:               "  pm.expect(data).to.have.property('latency_ms');",
 382:               "  pm.expect(data.latency_ms).to.be.a('number');",
 383:               "});",
 384:               "",
 385:               "// Store last translation for quick reuse in other requests",
 386:               "pm.collectionVariables.set('lastTranslation', data.translation);",
 387:               "pm.collectionVariables.set('lastProvider', data.provider);"

Translate sourceText. 2. pronunciation (pinyin). conversation (order the dish in sourceLang)\"\n}"
 420:         },

 436:       "name": "imageScan [data]",
 437:       "event": [
 438:         {
 439:           "listen": "test",
 440:           "script": {
 441:             "exec": [
 442:               "// Comprehensive tests for POST /api/imageScan endpoint",
 443:               "",
 444:               "// Test 1: Status code validation",
 445:               "pm.test(\"Status code is 200 (Success)\", function () {",
 446:               "    pm.response.to.have.status(200);",
 447:               "});",
 448:               "",
 449:               "// Test 2: Response body exists",
 450:               "pm.test(\"Response body is not empty\", function () {",
 451:               "    pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.not.be.empty;",
 452:               "});",
 453:               "",
 454:               "// Test 3: Response is valid JSON",
 455:               "pm.test(\"Response is valid JSON\", function () {",
 456:               "    pm.response.to.be.json;",
 457:               "});",
 458:               "",
 459:               "// Test 4: Response body structure validation",
 460:               "pm.test(\"Response has expected structure\", function () {",
 461:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 462:               "    pm.expect(jsonData).to.be.an('object');",
 463:               "});",
 464:               "",
 465:               "// Test 5: Check for success indicators",
 466:               "pm.test(\"Response indicates successful processing\", function () {",
 467:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 468:               "    // Check for common success patterns",
 469:               "    const hasSuccessField = jsonData.success === true || jsonData.status === \"success\";",
 470:               "    const hasData = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 471:               "    pm.expect(hasSuccessField || hasData).to.be.true;",
 472:               "});",
 473:               "",
 474:               "// Test 6: Extracted text validation",
 475:               "pm.test(\"Response contains extracted text data\", function () {",
 476:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 477:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 478:               "    pm.expect(textField).to.exist;",
 479:               "});",
 480:               "",
 481:               "// Test 7: Extracted text is not empty (if present)",
 482:               "pm.test(\"Extracted text is not empty string\", function () {",
 483:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 484:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 485:               "    if (textField !== undefined) {",
 486:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.not.equal(\"\");",
 487:               "    }",
 488:               "});",
 489:               "",
 490:               "// Test 8: Response time validation",
 491:               "pm.test(\"Image scan completes in reasonable time (under 10 seconds)\", function () {",
 492:               "    pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(10000);",
 493:               "});",
 494:               "",
 495:               "// Error Handling Tests",
 496:               "",
 497:               "// Test 9: Handle 400 Bad Request errors",
 498:               "if (pm.response.code === 400) {",
 499:               "    pm.test(\"Bad Request (400) includes error message\", function () {",
 500:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 501:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 502:               "    });",
 503:               "}",
 504:               "",
 505:               "// Test 10: Handle 401 Unauthorized errors",
 506:               "if (pm.response.code === 401) {",
 507:               "    pm.test(\"Unauthorized (401) includes authentication error\", function () {",
 508:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 509:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 510:               "    });",
 511:               "}",
 512:               "",
 513:               "// Test 11: Handle 413 Payload Too Large errors",
 514:               "if (pm.response.code === 413) {",
 515:               "    pm.test(\"Payload Too Large (413) indicates file size issue\", function () {",
 516:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 517:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 518:               "    });",
 519:               "}",
 520:               "",
 521:               "// Test 12: Handle 415 Unsupported Media Type errors",
 522:               "if (pm.response.code === 415) {",
 523:               "    pm.test(\"Unsupported Media Type (415) indicates format issue\", function () {",
 524:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 525:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 526:               "    });",
 527:               "}",
 528:               "",
 529:               "// Test 13: Handle 500 Internal Server Error",
 530:               "if (pm.response.code === 500) {",
 531:               "    pm.test(\"Internal Server Error (500) includes error details\", function () {",
 532:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 533:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.error || jsonData.message).to.exist;",
 534:               "    });",
 535:               "}",
 536:               "",
 537:               "// Content Validation Tests",
 538:               "",
 539:               "// Test 14: Check response schema consistency",
 540:               "pm.test(\"Response follows consistent schema\", function () {",
 541:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 542:               "    const hasStandardFields = (",
 543:               "        (jsonData.hasOwnProperty('success') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('status')) &&",
 544:               "        (jsonData.hasOwnProperty('text') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('extractedText') || ",
 545:               "         jsonData.hasOwnProperty('data') || jsonData.hasOwnProperty('error'))",
 546:               "    );",
 547:               "    pm.expect(hasStandardFields).to.be.true;",
 548:               "});",
 549:               "",
 550:               "// Test 15: Validate text content type",
 551:               "pm.test(\"Extracted text is string type\", function () {",
 552:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 553:               "    const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 554:               "    if (textField !== undefined && textField !== null) {",
 555:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.be.a('string');",
 556:               "    }",
 557:               "});",
 558:               "",
 559:               "// Test 16: Check for metadata (if applicable)",
 560:               "pm.test(\"Response may include metadata\", function () {",
 561:               "    const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 562:               "    // This is a soft check - metadata is optional",
 563:               "    if (jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language) {",
 564:               "        pm.expect(jsonData.metadata || jsonData.confidence || jsonData.language).to.exist;",
 565:               "    }",
 566:               "});",
 567:               "",
 568:               "// Test 17: Validate no unexpected null values in success response",
 569:               "if (pm.response.code === 200) {",
 570:               "    pm.test(\"Success response has no critical null values\", function () {",
 571:               "        const jsonData = pm.response.json();",
 572:               "        const textField = jsonData.text || jsonData.extractedText || jsonData.data || jsonData.result;",
 573:               "        pm.expect(textField).to.not.be.null;",
 574:               "    });",
 575:               "}"

 955:         ],
 956:         "body": {
 957:           "mode": "raw",
 958:           "raw": "{\n  \"prompt_id\": \"{{PROMPT_ID}}\",\n  \"prompt\": \"You are an Educational UI Composer for a mobile app. You OUTPUT STRUCTURED UI BLOCKS that a React Native app can render. Rules: - Output ONLY valid JSON. Use ONLY the block types defined below. - Prefer visual layouts over paragraphs. - Optimize for tutoring and visual symmetry. Minimum 3 blocks per response. - Every block must be self-contained and renderable. - If content is ambiguous, choose the most pedagogically useful representation. Allowed block types: 1. gridVocab 2. examples 3. callout 4. markdown Block schemas: gridVocab: { 'type': 'gridVocab', 'title': string, 'center': { 'text': string }, 'items': [ { 'hanzi': string, 'pinyin': string, 'tone': number, 'gloss': string } ], 'note': string (optional) } examples: { 'type': 'examples', 'title': string, 'items': [ { 'native': string, 'target': string } ] } callout: { 'type': 'callout', 'variant': 'tip' | 'note' | 'warning', 'markdown': string } markdown: { 'type': 'markdown', 'content': string } Tone: A knowledgable friend, speaking as natives would, not from a textbook - Pinyin (space between sylables, in string, for easier reading) - Show examples with Pinyin - Valid JSON EXAMPLE: [ { \\\"type\\\": \\\"gridVocab\\\", \\\"title\\\": \\\"Cool/Cold in Food Context\\\", \\\"center\\\": { \\\"text\\\": \\\"凉\\\" }, \\\"items\\\": [ { \\\"hanzi\\\": \\\"冷饮\\\", \\\"pinyin\\\": \\\"lěng yǐn\\\", \\\"tone\\\": [3, 3], \\\"gloss\\\": \\\"Cold drink\\\" }, { \\\"hanzi\\\": \\\"凉菜\\\", \\\"pinyin\\\": \\\"liáng cài\\\", \\\"tone\\\": [2, 4], \\\"gloss\\\": \\\"Cold dish\\\" }, { \\\"hanzi\\\": \\\"凉面\\\", \\\"pinyin\\\": \\\"liáng miàn\\\", \\\"tone\\\": [2, 4], \\\"gloss\\\": \\\"Cold noodles\\\" } ], \\\"note\\\": \\\"Common food terms using 凉 in Chinese cuisine\\\" } ] - Output text outside JSON\",\n  \"enabled\": true\n}"
 959:         },
 960:         "url": {
 961:           "raw": "{{SERVER_BASE_URL}}/api/kiaspora/promptConfig",
 962:           "host": [
 963:             "{{SERVER_BASE_URL}}"
 964:           ],

 967:             "kiaspora",

 969:           ]
 970:         }
