## File: src/parse/imdb-detail.controller.ts
``````typescript
  1: import { Controller, Get, Query, Req, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
  2: import type { Request } from 'express';
  3: import { readFile, unlink } from 'node:fs/promises';
  4: import path from 'node:path';
  5: import * as cheerio from 'cheerio';
  6: import type { Element } from 'domhandler';
  7: 
  8: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
  9: import type { TraceRequest } from '../common/trace-id.middleware';
 10: 
 11: type ParseHtmlResponse = {
 12:   statusCode: number;
 13:   url: string;
 14:   filePath: string | null;
 15:   errors: string[];
 16:   latency: number;
 17:   size: number;
 18: };
 19: 
 20: type ImdbDetailResponse = {
 21:   requestId: string;
 22:   statusCode: number;
 23:   dataSource: 'imdb';
 24:   imdbId: string;
 25:   info: {
 26:     title: string;
 27:     year: string;
 28:     rated: string;
 29:     released: string;
 30:     runtime: string;
 31:     genre: string;
 32:     director: string;
 33:     writers: string;
 34:     actors: string;
 35:     plot: string;
 36:     language: string;
 37:     country: string;
 38:     poster: string;
 39:     imdbId: string;
 40:   };
 41:   errors: string[];
 42: };
 43: 
 44: function parseRuntimeToMinutes(runtimeText: string): string {
 45: 
 46:   const t = runtimeText.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
 47: 
 48:   const hm = /^(?:(\d+)\s*h)?\s*(?:(\d+)\s*m)?$/.exec(t);
 49:   if (hm) {
 50:     const h = hm[1] ? Number(hm[1]) : 0;
 51:     const m = hm[2] ? Number(hm[2]) : 0;
 52:     const mins = h * 60 + m;
 53:     if (mins > 0) return String(mins);
 54:   }
 55: 
 56:   const mm = /^(\d+)\s*m$/.exec(t);
 57:   if (mm) return mm[1];
 58: 
 59:   const hOnly = /^(\d+)\s*h$/.exec(t);
 60:   if (hOnly) return String(Number(hOnly[1]) * 60);
 61: 
 62:   return '';
 63: }
 64: 
 65: function usDateToDMY(s: string): string {
 66:   // input: "May 22, 2025 (United States)" or "May 22, 2025"
 67:   const base = (s.split('(')[0] ?? s).trim();
 68:   const m = /^([A-Za-z]+)\s+(\d{1,2}),\s*(\d{4})$/.exec(base);
 69:   if (!m) return base;
 70: 
 71:   const monthMap: Record<string, string> = {
 72:     january: 'Jan',
 73:     february: 'Feb',
 74:     march: 'Mar',
 75:     april: 'Apr',
 76:     may: 'May',
 77:     june: 'Jun',
 78:     july: 'Jul',
 79:     august: 'Aug',
 80:     september: 'Sep',
 81:     october: 'Oct',
 82:     november: 'Nov',
 83:     december: 'Dec',
 84:   };
 85:   const month = monthMap[m[1].toLowerCase()] || m[1].slice(0, 3);
 86:   const day = m[2].padStart(2, '0');
 87:   const year = m[3];
 88:   return `${day} ${month} ${year}`;
 89: }
 90: 
 91: function isoDateToDMY(s: string): string {
 92: 
 93:   const m = /^(\d{4})-(\d{2})-(\d{2})$/.exec(s.trim());
 94:   if (!m) return '';
 95:   const months = ['Jan', 'Feb', 'Mar', 'Apr', 'May', 'Jun', 'Jul', 'Aug', 'Sep', 'Oct', 'Nov', 'Dec'];
 96:   const monthIdx = Number(m[2]) - 1;
 97:   if (monthIdx < 0 || monthIdx > 11) return '';
 98:   return `${m[3]} ${months[monthIdx]} ${m[1]}`;
 99: }
100: 
101: function isoDurationToRuntimeText(isoDuration: string): string {
102:   // input examples: "PT1H43M", "PT103M", "PT2H"
103:   const m = /^PT(?:(\d+)H)?(?:(\d+)M)?$/i.exec(isoDuration.trim());
104:   if (!m) return '';
105:   const h = m[1] ? Number(m[1]) : 0;
106:   const min = m[2] ? Number(m[2]) : 0;
107:   if (!h && !min) return '';
108:   return `${h ? `${h}h` : ''}${h && min ? ' ' : ''}${min ? `${min}m` : ''}`.trim();
109: }
110: 
111: function textOf($: cheerio.CheerioAPI, sel: string): string {
112:   return $(sel).first().text().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
113: }
114: 
115: function firstLinkTextByHrefIncludes($: cheerio.CheerioAPI, hrefIncludes: string): string {
116:   const a = $(`a[href*="${hrefIncludes}"]`).first();
117:   return a.text().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
118: }
119: 
120: function linkTextsByHrefIncludes($: cheerio.CheerioAPI, hrefIncludes: string): string[] {
121:   return $(`a[href*="${hrefIncludes}"]`)
122:     .map((_, a) => $(a).text().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim())
123:     .get()
124:     .filter(Boolean);
125: }
126: 
127: type ImdbJsonLd = {
128:   genre?: string[] | string;
129:   datePublished?: string;
130:   duration?: string;
131:   description?: string;
132:   image?: string;
133:   contentRating?: string;
134: };
135: 
136: function firstMovieJsonLd($: cheerio.CheerioAPI): ImdbJsonLd | null {
137:   const scripts = $('script[type="application/ld+json"]').toArray();
138: 
139:   const toList = (node: any): any[] => {
140:     if (!node) return [];
141:     if (Array.isArray(node)) return node;
142:     if (Array.isArray(node['@graph'])) return node['@graph'];
143:     return [node];
144:   };
145: 
146:   const isMovieType = (t: unknown): boolean => {
147:     if (typeof t === 'string') return t.toLowerCase() === 'movie';
148:     if (Array.isArray(t)) return t.some((x) => typeof x === 'string' && x.toLowerCase() === 'movie');
149:     return false;
150:   };
151: 
152:   for (const s of scripts) {
153:     const raw = $(s).text().trim();
154:     if (!raw) continue;
155:     try {
156:       const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
157:       const items = toList(parsed);
158:       const movie = items.find((it) => isMovieType(it?.['@type']));
159:       if (movie) return movie as ImdbJsonLd;
160:     } catch {
161: 
162:     }
163:   }
164: 
165:   return null;
166: }
167: 
168: function listLinkTexts($root: cheerio.Cheerio<Element>, $: cheerio.CheerioAPI): string[] {
169:   return $root
170:     .find('a')
171:     .map((_, a) => $(a).text().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim())
172:     .get()
173:     .filter(Boolean);
174: }
175: 
176: function findPeopleByLabel(
177:   $: cheerio.CheerioAPI,
178:   label: string,
179: ): string {
180: 
181:   const $list = $('[data-testid="title-pc-list"]').first();
182:   if (!$list.length) return '';
183: 
184:   const $li = $list
185:     .find('li[data-testid="title-pc-principal-credit"]')
186:     .filter((_, li) => {
187:       const t = $(li).find('span.ipc-metadata-list-item__label').first().text().trim();
188:       return t === label;
189:     })
190:     .first();
191: 
192:   if (!$li.length) return '';
193: 
194:   const names = listLinkTexts($li, $);
195:   return names.join(', ');
196: }
197: 
198: function findStars($: cheerio.CheerioAPI): string {
199:   // Stars label is an <a> with text "Stars" (not a span label)
200:   const $list = $('[data-testid="title-pc-list"]').first();
201:   if (!$list.length) return '';
202: 
203:   const $li = $list
204:     .find('li[data-testid="title-pc-principal-credit"]')
205:     .filter((_, li) => {
206:       const t = $(li).find('a.ipc-metadata-list-item__label').first().text().trim();
207:       return t === 'Stars';
208:     })
209:     .first();
210: 
211:   if (!$li.length) return '';
212: 
213:   const names = listLinkTexts($li, $).filter((n) => n !== 'Stars');
214:   return names.join(', ');
215: }
216: 
217: @Controller('api/parse')
218: @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
219: export class ImdbDetailController {
220:   @Get('imdbDetail')
221:   async imdbDetail(@Query('imdbId') imdbIdRaw: string | undefined, @Req() req: TraceRequest) {
222:     const requestId =
223:       (req.traceId ||
224:         (req.headers['x-trace-id'] as string | undefined) ||
225:         (req.headers['x-request-id'] as string | undefined) ||
226:         'unknown') + '';
227: 
228:     const imdbId = (imdbIdRaw ?? '').trim();
229: 
230:     const blank: ImdbDetailResponse = {
231:       requestId,
232:       statusCode: 400,
233:       dataSource: 'imdb',
234:       imdbId,
235:       info: {
236:         title: '',
237:         year: '',
238:         rated: '',
239:         released: '',
240:         runtime: '',
241:         genre: '',
242:         director: '',
243:         writers: '',
244:         actors: '',
245:         plot: '',
246:         language: '',
247:         country: '',
248:         poster: '',
249:         imdbId,
250:       },
251:       errors: [],
252:     };
253: 
254:     if (!/^tt\d+$/.test(imdbId)) {
255:       return {
256:         ...blank,
257:         statusCode: 400,
258:         errors: ['Invalid imdbId (expected tt123...)'],
259:       };
260:     }
261: 
262:     // Build local base URL (supports proxies)
263:     const proto =
264:       (req.headers['x-forwarded-proto'] as string | undefined)?.split(',')[0]?.trim() ||
265:       (req as any).protocol ||
266:       'http';
267: 
268:     const host =
269:       (req.headers['x-forwarded-host'] as string | undefined)?.split(',')[0]?.trim() ||
270:       (req as any).get?.('host') ||
271:       req.headers.host;
272: 
273:     const base = `${proto}://${host}`;
274: 
275:     const targetUrl = `https://www.imdb.com/title/${imdbId}`;
276:     const localParseUrl = `${base}/api/parse/html?url=${encodeURIComponent(targetUrl)}`;
277: 
278:     const auth = (req.headers?.authorization ?? (req.headers as any)?.Authorization) as
279:       | string
280:       | undefined;
281: 
282:     let parseResp: ParseHtmlResponse;
283:     try {
284:       const r = await fetch(localParseUrl, {
285:         redirect: 'follow',
286:         headers: {
287:           ...(auth ? { Authorization: auth } : {}),
288:           ...(req.traceId ? { 'x-trace-id': req.traceId } : {}),
289:         },
290:       });
291:       parseResp = (await r.json()) as ParseHtmlResponse;
292:     } catch (e: any) {
293:       return {
294:         ...blank,
295:         statusCode: 500,
296:         errors: [String(e?.message ?? e)],
297:       };
298:     }
299: 
300:     if (!parseResp.filePath || (parseResp.errors?.length ?? 0) > 0) {
301:       return {
302:         ...blank,
303:         statusCode: parseResp.statusCode || 500,
304:         errors: parseResp.errors?.length ? parseResp.errors : ['No filePath returned'],
305:       };
306:     }
307: 
308:     if (!parseResp.filePath.startsWith('temp/')) {
309:       return {
310:         ...blank,
311:         statusCode: 400,
312:         errors: ['Invalid filePath (must start with temp/)'],
313:       };
314:     }
315: 
316:     const absPath = path.join(process.cwd(), parseResp.filePath);
317: 
318:     let html = '';
319:     try {
320:       html = await readFile(absPath, 'utf8');
321:     } catch (e: any) {
322:       return {
323:         ...blank,
324:         statusCode: 500,
325:         errors: [String(e?.message ?? e)],
326:       };
327:     }
328: 
329:     const $ = cheerio.load(html);
330:     const bodyClone = $('body').clone();
331:     bodyClone.find('script, style, noscript').remove();
332:     const bodyText = bodyClone.text().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
333:     const jsonLd = firstMovieJsonLd($);
334: 
335: 
336:     const title = textOf($, 'h1[data-testid="hero__pageTitle"]');
337:     const yearRaw =
338:       firstLinkTextByHrefIncludes($, '/releaseinfo/?ref_=tt_ov_rdat') ||
339:       firstLinkTextByHrefIncludes($, '/releaseinfo/');
340:     const year = yearRaw || (jsonLd?.datePublished ? jsonLd.datePublished.slice(0, 4) : '');
341:     const ratedRaw =
342:       firstLinkTextByHrefIncludes($, '/parentalguide/?ref_=tt_ov_pg#certificates') ||
343:       firstLinkTextByHrefIncludes($, '/parentalguide/');
344:     const rated = /^[A-Z0-9-]{1,10}$/.test(ratedRaw)
345:       ? ratedRaw
346:       : /^[A-Z0-9-]{1,10}$/.test(jsonLd?.contentRating ?? '')
347:         ? (jsonLd?.contentRating ?? '')
348:         : '';
349: 
350:     const heroItems = $('h1[data-testid="hero__pageTitle"]')
351:       .first()
352:       .parent()
353:       .find('ul.ipc-inline-list li.ipc-inline-list__item')
354:       .map((_, li) => $(li).text().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim())
355:       .get()
356:       .filter(Boolean);
357: 
358:     let runtimeText =
359:       heroItems.find((x) => /\b\d+\s*h(?:\s*\d+\s*m)?\b/i.test(x) || /^\d+\s*m$/i.test(x)) || '';
360: 
361:     if (!runtimeText && jsonLd?.duration) {
362:       runtimeText = isoDurationToRuntimeText(jsonLd.duration);
363:     }
364: 
365:     if (!runtimeText) {
366:       const runtimeMatch = bodyText.match(/\bRuntime\b[^A-Za-z0-9]*(\d+\s*h(?:\s*\d+\s*m)?|\d+\s*m)\b/i);
367:       runtimeText = runtimeMatch?.[1]?.trim() ?? '';
368:     }
369: 
370:     const runtime = parseRuntimeToMinutes(runtimeText);
371: 
372:     // ---- genres (Storyline section) ----
373:     const genreParts = linkTextsByHrefIncludes($, 'ref_=tt_stry_gnr').length
374:       ? linkTextsByHrefIncludes($, 'ref_=tt_stry_gnr')
375:       : linkTextsByHrefIncludes($, '/search/title/?genres=').length
376:         ? linkTextsByHrefIncludes($, '/search/title/?genres=')
377:         : linkTextsByHrefIncludes($, 'explore=genres');
378:     const genreFromJsonLd = Array.isArray(jsonLd?.genre)
379:       ? jsonLd.genre
380:       : typeof jsonLd?.genre === 'string'
381:         ? [jsonLd.genre]
382:         : [];
383:     const genre = [...new Set((genreParts.length ? genreParts : genreFromJsonLd).filter(Boolean))].join(', ');
384: 
385: 
386:     let plot = $('[data-testid="storyline-plot-summary"] .ipc-html-content-inner-div')
387:       .first()
388:       .text()
389:       .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
390:       .trim();
391: 
392:     if (!plot) {
393:       plot = textOf($, '[data-testid="plot-xl"]');
394:     }
395:     if (!plot) {
396:       plot = textOf($, '[data-testid="plot-l"]');
397:     }
398:     if (!plot && jsonLd?.description) {
399:       plot = jsonLd.description.trim();
400:     }
401:     plot = plot.replace(/\s+Read all$/i, '').trim();
402: 
403:     if (!plot) {
404:       const m = bodyText.match(/([A-Z][^.?!]{40,}[.?!])\s*Read all/i);
405:       if (m?.[1]) plot = m[1].trim();
406:     }
407: 
408:     // ---- details: release / country / language ----
409:     let released = '';
410:     const releaseTexts = $('[data-testid="title-details-releasedate"] a')
411:       .map((_, a) => $(a).text().replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim())
412:       .get()
413:       .filter(Boolean);
414:     const releaseText =
415:       releaseTexts.find((t) => /\b[A-Z][a-z]+\s+\d{1,2},\s*\d{4}\b/.test(t)) || '';
416:     if (releaseText) {
417:       released = usDateToDMY(releaseText);
418:     } else if (jsonLd?.datePublished) {
419:       released = isoDateToDMY(jsonLd.datePublished);
420:     }
421: 
422:     const country = textOf($, '[data-testid="title-details-origin"] a.ipc-metadata-list-item__list-content-item');
423:     const language = textOf($, '[data-testid="title-details-languages"] a.ipc-metadata-list-item__list-content-item');
424: 
425: 
426:     const director = findPeopleByLabel($, 'Director') || findPeopleByLabel($, 'Creator');
427: 
428:     const writers = findPeopleByLabel($, 'Writers') || findPeopleByLabel($, 'Writer');
429:     const actors = findStars($);
430: 
431: 
432:     const poster =
433:       $('meta[property="og:image"]').attr('content')?.trim() ||
434:       jsonLd?.image?.trim() ||
435:       $('img.ipc-image[src*="m.media-amazon.com/images"]').first().attr('src')?.trim() ||
436:       '';
437: 
438:     // ---- enforce “must be found in HTML” ----
439:     const errors: string[] = [];
440:     const require = (k: string, v: string) => {
441:       if (!v) errors.push(`Missing field from HTML: ${k}`);
442:     };
443: 
444:     require('title', title);
445:     require('year', year);
446:     require('rated', rated);
447:     require('runtime', runtime);
448:     require('genre', genre);
449:     require('plot', plot);
450:     require('poster', poster);
451:     require('director_or_creator', director);
452:     require('writers', writers);
453:     require('actors', actors);
454: 
455:     const resp: ImdbDetailResponse = {
456:       requestId,
457:       statusCode: errors.length ? 206 : 200,
458:       dataSource: 'imdb',
459:       imdbId,
460:       info: {
461:         title,
462:         year,
463:         rated,
464:         released,
465:         runtime,
466:         genre,
467:         director,
468:         writers,
469:         actors,
470:         plot,
471:         language,
472:         country,
473:         poster,
474:         imdbId,
475:       },
476:       errors,
477:     };
478: 
479:     try {
480:       await unlink(absPath);
481:     } catch {
482: 
483:     }
484: 
485:     return resp;
486:   }
487: }
``````

## File: src/parse/imdb-search.controller.ts
``````typescript
  1: import { Controller, Get, Query, Req, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
  2: import type { Request } from 'express';
  3: import { readFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
  4: import path from 'node:path';
  5: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
  6: import type { TraceRequest } from '../common/trace-id.middleware';
  7: 
  8: type ParseHtmlResponse = {
  9:   statusCode: number;
 10:   url: string;
 11:   filePath: string | null;
 12:   errors: string[];
 13:   latency: number;
 14:   size: number;
 15: };
 16: 
 17: type ImdbSearchItem = {
 18:   imdbId: string;
 19:   title: string;
 20:   year: number;
 21: };
 22: 
 23: type ImdbSearchResponse = {
 24:   statusCode: number;
 25:   query: string;
 26:   url: string;
 27:   filePath: string | null;
 28:   errors: string[];
 29:   latency: number;
 30:   size: number;
 31:   results: ImdbSearchItem[];
 32: };
 33: 
 34: function minimalEntityDecode(s: string): string {
 35:   return s
 36:     .replace(/&amp;/g, '&')
 37:     .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
 38:     .replace(/&#39;/g, "'")
 39:     .replace(/&lt;/g, '<')
 40:     .replace(/&gt;/g, '>');
 41: }
 42: 
 43: function parseImdbFindHtml(html: string): ImdbSearchItem[] {
 44: 
 45:   const re =
 46:     /href="\/title\/(tt\d+)\/[^"]*"[^>]*>.*?<h3[^>]*class="ipc-title__text"[^>]*>(.*?)<\/h3>.*?<span[^>]*cli-title-metadata-item[^>]*>(\d{4})<\/span>/gs;
 47: 
 48:   const out: ImdbSearchItem[] = [];
 49:   let m: RegExpExecArray | null;
 50: 
 51:   while ((m = re.exec(html)) !== null) {
 52:     const imdbId = m[1];
 53:     const titleRaw = m[2] ?? '';
 54:     const yearRaw = m[3];
 55: 
 56:     out.push({
 57:       imdbId,
 58:       title: minimalEntityDecode(titleRaw),
 59:       year: Number(yearRaw),
 60:     });
 61:   }
 62: 
 63:   return out;
 64: }
 65: 
 66: @Controller('api/parse')
 67: export class ImdbSearchController {
 68:   @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
 69:   @Get('imdbSearch')
 70:   async imdbSearch(@Query('query') query: string | undefined, @Req() req: TraceRequest) {
 71:     const auth = (req.headers?.authorization ?? req.headers?.Authorization) as string | undefined;
 72:     const started = Date.now();
 73: 
 74:     const q = (query ?? '').trim();
 75:     if (!q) {
 76:       const latency = Date.now() - started;
 77:       const resp: ImdbSearchResponse = {
 78:         statusCode: 400,
 79:         query: q,
 80:         url: '',
 81:         filePath: null,
 82:         errors: ['Missing required query param: query'],
 83:         latency,
 84:         size: 0,
 85:         results: [],
 86:       };
 87:       return resp;
 88:     }
 89: 
 90: 
 91:     const imdbUrl = `https://www.imdb.com/find/?q=${encodeURIComponent(q)}`;
 92: 
 93:     const proto =
 94:       (req.headers['x-forwarded-proto'] as string | undefined)?.split(',')[0]?.trim() ||
 95:       req.protocol ||
 96:       'http';
 97: 
 98:     const host =
 99:       (req.headers['x-forwarded-host'] as string | undefined)?.split(',')[0]?.trim() ||
100:       req.get('host');
101: 
102:     const base = `${proto}://${host}`;
103:     const localParseUrl = `${base}/api/parse/html?url=${encodeURIComponent(imdbUrl)}`;
104: 
105:     let parseResp: ParseHtmlResponse;
106:     try {
107:       const r = await fetch(localParseUrl, {
108:         redirect: 'follow',
109:         headers: {
110:             ...(auth ? { Authorization: auth } : {}),
111:             ...(req.traceId ? { 'x-trace-id': req.traceId } : {}),
112:         },
113:       });
114:       parseResp = (await r.json()) as ParseHtmlResponse;
115:     } catch (e: any) {
116:       const latency = Date.now() - started;
117:       return {
118:         statusCode: 500,
119:         query: q,
120:         url: imdbUrl,
121:         filePath: null,
122:         errors: [e?.message ? String(e.message) : 'Failed to call /api/parse/html'],
123:         latency,
124:         size: 0,
125:         results: [],
126:       } satisfies ImdbSearchResponse;
127:     }
128: 
129:     if (!parseResp.filePath || parseResp.errors?.length) {
130:       const latency = Date.now() - started;
131:       return {
132:         statusCode: parseResp.statusCode || 500,
133:         query: q,
134:         url: parseResp.url || imdbUrl,
135:         filePath: parseResp.filePath ?? null,
136:         errors: parseResp.errors?.length ? parseResp.errors : ['No filePath returned'],
137:         latency,
138:         size: parseResp.size ?? 0,
139:         results: [],
140:       } satisfies ImdbSearchResponse;
141:     }
142: 
143: 
144:     if (!parseResp.filePath.startsWith('temp/')) {
145:       const latency = Date.now() - started;
146:       return {
147:         statusCode: 400,
148:         query: q,
149:         url: parseResp.url || imdbUrl,
150:         filePath: parseResp.filePath,
151:         errors: ['Invalid filePath (must start with temp/)'],
152:         latency,
153:         size: 0,
154:         results: [],
155:       } satisfies ImdbSearchResponse;
156:     }
157: 
158:     const absPath = path.join(process.cwd(), parseResp.filePath);
159: 
160:     let html = '';
161:     try {
162:       html = await readFile(absPath, 'utf8');
163:     } catch (e: any) {
164:       const latency = Date.now() - started;
165:       return {
166:         statusCode: 500,
167:         query: q,
168:         url: parseResp.url || imdbUrl,
169:         filePath: parseResp.filePath,
170:         errors: [e?.message ? String(e.message) : 'Failed to read saved HTML file'],
171:         latency,
172:         size: 0,
173:         results: [],
174:       } satisfies ImdbSearchResponse;
175:     }
176: 
177:     const results = parseImdbFindHtml(html);
178:     const latency = Date.now() - started;
179: 
180:     return {
181:       statusCode: 200,
182:       query: q,
183:       url: parseResp.url || imdbUrl,
184:       filePath: parseResp.filePath,
185:       errors: [],
186:       latency,
187:       size: parseResp.size ?? Buffer.byteLength(html, 'utf8'),
188:       results,
189:     } satisfies ImdbSearchResponse;
190:   }
191: }
``````

``````typescript
  1: import { Controller, Get, Query, Req, Res, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
  2: import type { Request, Response } from 'express';
  3: import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
  4: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
  5: 
  6: const JSON_HEADERS = { 'content-type': 'application/json' };
  7: const CORS_HEADERS = {
  8:   'access-control-allow-origin': '*',
  9:   'access-control-allow-methods': 'GET,OPTIONS',
 10:   'access-control-allow-headers': 'content-type, authorization, x-trace-id',
 11: };
 12: 
 13: function makeError({
 14:   source,
 15:   message,
 16:   code,
 17:   details,
 18: }: {
 19:   source: string;
 20:   message: string;
 21:   code?: any;
 22:   details?: any;
 23: }) {
 24:   const out: any = { source, message };
 25:   if (code !== undefined && code !== null) out.code = code;
 26:   if (details !== undefined && details !== null) out.details = details;
 27:   return out;
 28: }
 29: 
 30: function toCamelKey(key: string) {
 31:   return String(key)
 32:     .replace(/^[A-Z]/, (m) => m.toLowerCase())
 33:     .replace(/[_-\s]+([a-zA-Z0-9])/g, (_, c) => c.toUpperCase());
 34: }
 35: 
 36: function camelizeValue(value: any): any {
 37:   if (Array.isArray(value)) return value.map(camelizeValue);
 38:   if (value && typeof value === 'object' && !Array.isArray(value)) {
 39:     const out: any = {};
 40:     for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(value)) out[toCamelKey(k)] = camelizeValue(v);
 41:     return out;
 42:   }
 43:   return value;
 44: }
 45: 
 46: function parseYearValue(raw: any): number | null {
 47:   if (typeof raw === 'number' && Number.isFinite(raw)) {
 48:     return Math.trunc(raw);
 49:   }
 50: 
 51:   const text = String(raw ?? '').trim();
 52:   if (!text) return null;
 53: 
 54:   // OMDb can return ranges like "2024-", "2024–", or "2024-2026".
 55:   const rangeMatch = text.match(/^(\d{4})\s*[-–]/);
 56:   if (rangeMatch) {
 57:     const year = parseInt(rangeMatch[1], 10);
 58:     return Number.isFinite(year) ? year : null;
 59:   }
 60: 
 61:   const match = text.match(/\b(\d{4})\b/);
 62:   if (!match) return null;
 63: 
 64:   const year = parseInt(match[1], 10);
 65:   return Number.isFinite(year) ? year : null;
 66: }
 67: 
 68: function toCanonicalSearchItem(raw: any): { tconst: string; title: string; year: number } | null {
 69:   if (!raw || typeof raw !== 'object' || Array.isArray(raw)) return null;
 70: 
 71:   const normalized = camelizeValue(raw);
 72: 
 73:   const tconst = String(
 74:     normalized.tconst ?? normalized.imdbId ?? normalized.imdbID ?? (raw as any).imdbID ?? '',
 75:   ).trim();
 76:   const title = String(normalized.title ?? '').trim();
 77: 
 78:   if (!tconst || !title) return null;
 79: 
 80:   const year = parseYearValue(normalized.year);
 81:   if (year === null) return null;
 82: 
 83:   return { tconst, title, year };
 84: }
 85: 
 86: function normalizeItemsToCanonical(items: any): Array<{ tconst: string; title: string; year: number }> {
 87:   if (!Array.isArray(items)) return [];
 88:   return items
 89:     .map((item) => toCanonicalSearchItem(item))
 90:     .filter((item): item is { tconst: string; title: string; year: number } => Boolean(item));
 91: }
 92: 
 93: function truncate(s: any, n = 800) {
 94:   if (typeof s !== 'string') return s;
 95:   return s.length > n ? `${s.slice(0, n)}…` : s;
 96: }
 97: 
 98: function extractErrMessage(err: any) {
 99:   if (!err) return 'unknown error';
100:   if (typeof err.message === 'string' && err.message.trim()) return err.message;
101:   return String(err);
102: }
103: 
104: function stringifyError(err: any) {
105:   try {
106:     return {
107:       message: err?.message ? String(err.message) : String(err),
108:       name: err?.name ?? null,
109:       stack: err?.stack ?? null,
110:       cause: err?.cause ? stringifyError(err.cause) : null,
111:     };
112:   } catch {
113:     return { message: String(err) };
114:   }
115: }
116: 
117: function parseTitleWithOptionalYearSuffix(raw: string) {
118:   const trimmed = String(raw || '').trim();
119:   const match = trimmed.match(/^(.*\S)\s+(\d{4})$/);
120:   if (!match) return { title: trimmed, year: null as number | null };
121: 
122:   const year = parseInt(match[2], 10);
123:   if (!Number.isFinite(year) || year < 1800 || year > 2100) {
124:     return { title: trimmed, year: null as number | null };
125:   }
126: 
127:   return { title: match[1].trim(), year };
128: }
129: 
130: @Controller('api/justus')
131: @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
132: export class TitleSearchController {
133:   @Get('titleSearch')
134:   async titleSearch(@Req() req: Request, @Res() res: Response, @Query() _qp: any) {
135:     const traceId =
136:       (req.header('x-trace-id') || req.header('X-Trace-Id'))?.trim() || randomUUID();
137: 
138:     const base = process.env.CF_WORKER_BASE_URL;
139:     if (!base) {
140:       const body = {
141:         requestId: traceId,
142:         statusCode: 500,
143:         dataSource: 'imdb',
144:         items: [],
145:         itemCount: 0,
146:         pageLimit: 0,
147:         errors: [
148:           makeError({
149:             source: 'config',
150:             message: 'Missing CF_WORKER_BASE_URL',
151:             code: 'MISSING_CF_WORKER_BASE_URL',
152:           }),
153:         ],
154:       };
155: 
156:       res.setHeader('x-trace-id', traceId);
157:       return res
158:         .status(500)
159:         .set({ ...JSON_HEADERS, ...CORS_HEADERS })
160:         .send(JSON.stringify(body));
161:     }
162: 
163: 
164:     const qs = req.url.split('?')[1] ?? '';
165:     const params = new URLSearchParams(qs);
166: 
167:     const incomingQuery = params.get('q') || params.get('query') || params.get('s') || '';
168:     const parsedQuery = parseTitleWithOptionalYearSuffix(incomingQuery);
169: 
170:     // Forward everything except dataSource; normalize search params for CF worker
171:     const upstreamParams = new URLSearchParams(params);
172:     upstreamParams.delete('dataSource');
173:     upstreamParams.delete('query');
174:     upstreamParams.delete('s');
175: 
176:     if (parsedQuery.title) {
177:       upstreamParams.set('q', parsedQuery.title);
178:     }
179: 
180:     if (parsedQuery.year !== null) {
181:       upstreamParams.set('year_min', String(parsedQuery.year));
182:       upstreamParams.set('year_max', String(parsedQuery.year));
183:       upstreamParams.delete('year');
184:     }
185: 
186:     const pageLimit = parseInt(params.get('limit') || '20', 10);
187:     const omdbQuery = parsedQuery.title;
188:     const omdbPageRaw = parseInt(params.get('page') || '', 10);
189: 
190:     const targetUrl =
191:       `${base.replace(/\/$/, '')}/search/title` +
192:       (upstreamParams.toString() ? `?${upstreamParams.toString()}` : '');
193: 
194:     const errors: any[] = [];
195:     let items: any[] = [];
196:     let upstreamStatus = 200;
197:     let dataSource: 'imdb' | 'omdb' = 'imdb';
198:     let upstreamDebug: any = null;
199: 
200:     async function fetchOmdbFallback() {
201:       const apiKey = process.env.OMDB_API_KEY;
202: 
203:       if (!apiKey) {
204:         return {
205:           ok: false,
206:           status: 500,
207:           error: makeError({
208:             source: 'omdb',
209:             message: 'Missing OMDB_API_KEY',
210:             code: 'MISSING_OMDB_API_KEY',
211:           }),
212:         };
213:       }
214: 
215:       if (!String(omdbQuery).trim()) {
216:         return {
217:           ok: false,
218:           status: 400,
219:           error: makeError({
220:             source: 'omdb',
221:             message: 'Missing search query for OMDb fallback',
222:             code: 'MISSING_OMDB_QUERY',
223:           }),
224:         };
225:       }
226: 
227:       const omdbParams = new URLSearchParams({
228:         apikey: apiKey,
229:         s: String(omdbQuery).trim(),
230:       });
231: 
232:       if (Number.isInteger(omdbPageRaw) && omdbPageRaw > 0) {
233:         omdbParams.set('page', String(omdbPageRaw));
234:       }
235: 
236:       const omdbUrl = `http://www.omdbapi.com/?${omdbParams.toString()}`;
237: 
238:       try {
239:         const resp = await fetch(omdbUrl, {
240:           method: 'GET',
241:           headers: { 'x-trace-id': traceId },
242:         });
243: 
244:         const text = await resp.text().catch(() => '');
245:         let parsed: any = {};
246: 
247:         try {
248:           parsed = text ? JSON.parse(text) : {};
249:         } catch (e) {
250:           return {
251:             ok: false,
252:             status: 502,
253:             error: makeError({
254:               source: 'omdb',
255:               message: `OMDb JSON parse failed: ${extractErrMessage(e)}`,
256:               code: 'OMDB_JSON_PARSE_FAILED',
257:               details: { bodySnippet: truncate(text), omdbUrl },
258:             }),
259:           };
260:         }
261: 
262:         if (!resp.ok) {
263:           return {
264:             ok: false,
265:             status: resp.status,
266:             error: makeError({
267:               source: 'omdb',
268:               message: `OMDb HTTP ${resp.status} ${resp.statusText || ''}`.trim(),
269:               code: resp.status,
270:               details: { omdbUrl, bodySnippet: truncate(text) },
271:             }),
272:           };
273:         }
274: 
275:         if (String(parsed?.Response || '').toLowerCase() === 'false') {
276:           return {
277:             ok: false,
278:             status: 404,
279:             error: makeError({
280:               source: 'omdb',
281:               message: String(parsed?.Error || 'OMDb returned no results'),
282:               code: 'OMDB_NO_RESULTS',
283:               details: { omdbUrl },
284:             }),
285:           };
286:         }
287: 
288:         const found = Array.isArray(parsed?.Search) ? parsed.Search : [];
289: 
290:         return {
291:           ok: true,
292:           status: 200,
293:           items: normalizeItemsToCanonical(pageLimit > 0 ? found.slice(0, pageLimit) : found),
294:         };
295:       } catch (err) {
296:         return {
297:           ok: false,
298:           status: 502,
299:           error: makeError({
300:             source: 'omdb',
301:             message: `OMDb fetch failed: ${extractErrMessage(err)}`,
302:             code: 'OMDB_FETCH_FAILED',
303:             details: { error: stringifyError(err), omdbUrl },
304:           }),
305:         };
306:       }
307:     }
308: 
309: 
310:     try {
311:       const upstream = await fetch(targetUrl, {
312:         method: 'GET',
313:         headers: { 'x-trace-id': traceId },
314:       });
315: 
316:       upstreamStatus = upstream.status;
317: 
318:       const text = await upstream.text().catch(() => '');
319: 
320:       upstreamDebug = {
321:         targetUrl,
322:         status: upstream.status,
323:         statusText: upstream.statusText,
324:         contentType: upstream.headers?.get?.('content-type') || null,
325:         bodySnippet: truncate(text),
326:       };
327: 
328:       if (!upstream.ok) {
329:         errors.push(
330:           makeError({
331:             source: 'upstream',
332:             message: `Upstream HTTP ${upstream.status} ${upstream.statusText || ''}`.trim(),
333:             code: upstream.status,
334:             details: upstreamDebug,
335:           }),
336:         );
337:       } else {
338:         let parsed: any = [];
339:         try {
340:           parsed = text ? JSON.parse(text) : [];
341:         } catch (e) {
342:           errors.push(
343:             makeError({
344:               source: 'upstream',
345:               message: `Upstream JSON parse failed: ${extractErrMessage(e)}`,
346:               code: 'UPSTREAM_JSON_PARSE_FAILED',
347:               details: upstreamDebug,
348:             }),
349:           );
350:           parsed = [];
351:         }
352: 
353:         if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
354:           items = normalizeItemsToCanonical(parsed);
355:         } else {
356:           errors.push(
357:             makeError({
358:               source: 'upstream',
359:               message: 'Unexpected upstream response shape',
360:               code: 'UPSTREAM_UNEXPECTED_SHAPE',
361:               details: upstreamDebug,
362:             }),
363:           );
364:         }
365:       }
366:     } catch (err: any) {
367:       upstreamStatus = 500;
368:       const msg = extractErrMessage(err);
369: 
370:       upstreamDebug = {
371:         targetUrl,
372:         error: stringifyError(err),
373:         message: msg,
374:         name: err?.name || null,
375:         cause: err?.cause ? stringifyError(err.cause) : null,
376:         hasCFWorkerBaseUrl: Boolean(process.env.CF_WORKER_BASE_URL),
377:       };
378: 
379:       errors.push(
380:         makeError({
381:           source: 'upstream',
382:           message: `Upstream fetch failed: ${msg}`,
383:           code: 'UPSTREAM_FETCH_FAILED',
384:           details: upstreamDebug,
385:         }),
386:       );
387:     }
388: 
389: 
390:     const shouldTryOmdbFallback =
391:       (!Array.isArray(items) || items.length === 0) && Boolean(String(omdbQuery).trim());
392: 
393:     if (shouldTryOmdbFallback) {
394:       const omdb = await fetchOmdbFallback();
395: 
396:       if (omdb.ok) {
397:         items = omdb.items || [];
398:         dataSource = 'omdb';
399:         upstreamStatus = 200;
400:         errors.length = 0;
401:       } else if (omdb.error) {
402:         errors.push(omdb.error);
403:       }
404:     }
405: 
406:     const finalStatus = Array.isArray(items) && items.length > 0 ? 200 : upstreamStatus;
407: 
408:     const responseBody = {
409:       requestId: traceId,
410:       statusCode: finalStatus,
411:       dataSource,
412:       items,
413:       itemCount: items.length,
414:       pageLimit,
415:       errors,
416:     };
417: 
418:     res.setHeader('x-trace-id', traceId);
419:     return res
420:       .status(finalStatus)
421:       .set({ ...JSON_HEADERS, ...CORS_HEADERS })
422:       .send(JSON.stringify(responseBody));
423:   }
424: }
``````

## File: src/justus/trailers.controller.ts
``````typescript
  1: import { Controller, Get, Query, Req, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
  2: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
  3: import type { Request } from 'express';
  4: import * as fs from 'node:fs/promises';
  5: import * as path from 'node:path';
  6: 
  7: type TraceRequest = Request & { traceId?: string };
  8: 
  9: type TitleDetailBody = {
 10:   requestId: string;
 11:   statusCode: number;
 12:   dataSource: 'imdb' | string;
 13:   imdbId: string;
 14:   info: any;
 15:   errors: any[];
 16:   traceId?: string;
 17:   timestamp?: string;
 18: };
 19: 
 20: type ApiResponse<T> = {
 21:   statusCode: number;
 22:   errors: string[];
 23:   latency: number;
 24:   size?: number;
 25:   data: T;
 26: };
 27: 
 28: function clampInt(n: number, min: number, max: number) {
 29:   return Math.max(min, Math.min(max, n));
 30: }
 31: 
 32: function parseLimit(raw: any) {
 33:   const n = Number(String(raw ?? '').trim() || '10');
 34:   if (!Number.isFinite(n)) return 10;
 35:   return clampInt(Math.floor(n), 1, 40);
 36: }
 37: 
 38: function sleep(ms: number) {
 39:   return new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));
 40: }
 41: 
 42: function randomDelayMs() {
 43: 
 44:   return 500 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 1001);
 45: }
 46: 
 47: function isValidImdbId(s: unknown): s is string {
 48:   return typeof s === 'string' && /^tt\d{5,12}$/.test(s);
 49: }
 50: 
 51: function uniq<T>(arr: T[]) {
 52:   return Array.from(new Set(arr));
 53: }
 54: 
 55: function log(traceId: string | undefined, ...args: any[]) {
 56:   const prefix = traceId ? `[trailers][${traceId}]` : `[trailers]`;
 57: 
 58:   console.log(prefix, ...args);
 59: }
 60: 
 61: @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
 62: @Controller('api/justus')
 63: export class TrailersController {
 64:   @Get('trailers')
 65:   async trailers(
 66:     @Query('limit') limitRaw: string | undefined,
 67:     @Req() req: TraceRequest,
 68:   ): Promise<
 69:     ApiResponse<{
 70:       inputFile: string;
 71:       outputFile: string;
 72:       limit: number;
 73:       candidateCount: number;
 74:       requestedCount: number;
 75:       writtenCount: number;
 76:       items: TitleDetailBody[];
 77:     } | null>
 78:   > {
 79:     const started = Date.now();
 80:     const limit = parseLimit(limitRaw);
 81:     const traceId = req.traceId;
 82: 
 83:     log(traceId, 'START', { limit });
 84: 
 85:     const auth = (req.headers?.authorization ?? (req.headers as any)?.Authorization) as string | undefined;
 86: 
 87:     const proto =
 88:       (req.headers['x-forwarded-proto'] as string | undefined)?.split(',')[0]?.trim() ||
 89:       (req as any).protocol ||
 90:       'http';
 91: 
 92:     const host =
 93:       (req.headers['x-forwarded-host'] as string | undefined)?.split(',')[0]?.trim() ||
 94:       (req as any).get?.('host');
 95: 
 96:     const base = `${proto}://${host}`;
 97:     log(traceId, 'BASE', base);
 98: 
 99: 
100:     const inRel = 'public/trailer_ids.json';
101:     const inAbs = path.join(process.cwd(), inRel);
102: 
103:     type TrailerIdRow = { imdb_id?: unknown; imdbId?: unknown; id?: unknown } | string;
104: 
105:     let rawJson: unknown;
106:     try {
107:       log(traceId, 'READ input', inRel, '=>', inAbs);
108:       const raw = await fs.readFile(inAbs, 'utf8');
109:       rawJson = JSON.parse(raw);
110:     } catch (e: any) {
111:       log(traceId, 'READ/PARSE FAIL', e?.message || e);
112:       return {
113:         statusCode: 500,
114:         errors: [e?.message ? String(e.message) : `Failed to read/parse ${inRel}`],
115:         latency: Date.now() - started,
116:         data: null,
117:       };
118:     }
119: 
120:     if (!Array.isArray(rawJson)) {
121:       return {
122:         statusCode: 400,
123:         errors: [`${inRel} must be a JSON array`],
124:         latency: Date.now() - started,
125:         data: null,
126:       };
127:     }
128: 
129:     const extracted = rawJson
130:       .map((row: TrailerIdRow) => {
131:         if (typeof row === 'string') return row;
132:         if (row && typeof row === 'object') {
133:           const r = row as any;
134:           return (r.imdb_id ?? r.imdbId ?? r.id) as unknown;
135:         }
136:         return undefined;
137:       })
138:       .filter(isValidImdbId);
139: 
140:     const candidates = uniq(extracted);
141: 
142:     if (candidates.length === 0) {
143:       return {
144:         statusCode: 400,
145:         errors: [`No valid IMDb ids found in ${inRel}`],
146:         latency: Date.now() - started,
147:         data: null,
148:       };
149:     }
150: 
151: 
152:     const targetIds = candidates.slice(0, limit);
153:     log(traceId, 'TARGETS', { requestedCount: targetIds.length, targetIds });
154: 
155: 
156:     const items: TitleDetailBody[] = [];
157: 
158:     for (let i = 0; i < targetIds.length; i++) {
159:       const imdbId = targetIds[i];
160: 
161: 
162:       const detailUrl = `${base}/api/justus/titleDetail?imdbId=${encodeURIComponent(imdbId)}`;
163: 
164:       const t0 = Date.now();
165:       log(traceId, `DETAIL ${i + 1}/${targetIds.length} -> FETCH start`, { imdbId });
166: 
167:       try {
168:         const ac = new AbortController();
169:         const timeoutMs = 20_000;
170:         const timer = setTimeout(() => ac.abort(), timeoutMs);
171: 
172:         const r = await fetch(detailUrl, {
173:           redirect: 'follow',
174:           signal: ac.signal,
175:           headers: {
176:             ...(auth ? { Authorization: auth } : {}),
177:             ...(traceId ? { 'x-trace-id': traceId } : {}),
178:           },
179:         }).finally(() => clearTimeout(timer));
180: 
181:         const httpStatus = r.status;
182:         const body = (await r.json()) as TitleDetailBody;
183: 
184:         const ms = Date.now() - t0;
185:         const hasErrors = Array.isArray(body?.errors) && body.errors.length > 0;
186: 
187: 
188:         const ok = (body?.statusCode === 200 || body?.statusCode === 206) && !hasErrors;
189: 
190:         log(traceId, `DETAIL ${i + 1}/${targetIds.length} <- FETCH done`, {
191:           imdbId,
192:           httpStatus,
193:           bodyStatusCode: body?.statusCode,
194:           ok,
195:           ms,
196:         });
197: 
198:         if (ok) items.push(body);
199:       } catch (e: any) {
200:         const ms = Date.now() - t0;
201:         log(traceId, `DETAIL ${i + 1}/${targetIds.length} FAIL`, {
202:           imdbId,
203:           ms,
204:           err: e?.name === 'AbortError' ? 'AbortError (timeout)' : e?.message || String(e),
205:         });
206:       }
207: 
208:       if (i < targetIds.length - 1) {
209:         const d = randomDelayMs();
210:         log(traceId, `DELAY ${i + 1}/${targetIds.length}`, { ms: d });
211:         await sleep(d);
212:       }
213:     }
214: 
215: 
216:     const outRel = 'public/trailers_list.json';
217:     const outAbs = path.join(process.cwd(), outRel);
218:     const tmpAbs = `${outAbs}.tmp`;
219: 
220:     await fs.mkdir(path.dirname(outAbs), { recursive: true });
221:     await fs.writeFile(tmpAbs, JSON.stringify(items, null, 2), 'utf8');
222:     await fs.rename(tmpAbs, outAbs);
223: 
224:     const latency = Date.now() - started;
225:     log(traceId, 'DONE', { requestedCount: targetIds.length, writtenCount: items.length, latencyMs: latency });
226: 
227:     return {
228:       statusCode: 200,
229:       errors: items.length ? [] : ['No items parsed; trailers_list.json written as empty array'],
230:       latency,
231:       size: items.length,
232:       data: {
233:         inputFile: inRel,
234:         outputFile: outRel,
235:         limit,
236:         candidateCount: candidates.length,
237:         requestedCount: targetIds.length,
238:         writtenCount: items.length,
239:         items,
240:       },
241:     };
242:   }
243: }
``````

## File: src/parse/parse.controller.ts
``````typescript
  1: import { Controller, Get, Query, Res, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
  2: import type { Response } from 'express';
  3: import { mkdir, writeFile } from 'node:fs/promises';
  4: import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
  5: import { URL } from 'node:url';
  6: import path from 'node:path';
  7: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
  8: import { chromium } from 'playwright';
  9: 
 10: @Controller('api/parse')
 11: export class ParseController {
 12:   @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
 13:   @Get('html')
 14:   async parseHtml(
 15:     @Query('url') urlStr: string | undefined,
 16:     @Res() res: Response,
 17:   ) {
 18:     const started = Date.now();
 19: 
 20:     if (!urlStr) {
 21:       return res.status(400).json({
 22:         ok: false,
 23:         error: 'Missing required query param: url',
 24:       });
 25:     }
 26: 
 27:     let url: URL;
 28:     try {
 29:       url = new URL(urlStr);
 30:     } catch {
 31:       return res.status(400).json({
 32:         ok: false,
 33:         error: 'Invalid url',
 34:       });
 35:     }
 36: 
 37:     const TIMEOUT_MS = 45000;
 38:     const SCROLL_MS = 6000;
 39: 
 40:     const browser = await chromium.launch({
 41:       headless: true,
 42:       args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'],
 43:     });
 44: 
 45:     try {
 46:       const context = await browser.newContext({
 47:         userAgent: 'Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7)',
 48:         locale: 'en-US',
 49:         viewport: { width: 1400, height: 900 },
 50:       });
 51: 
 52:       const page = await context.newPage();
 53: 
 54:       const networkLog: any[] = [];
 55: 
 56:       page.on('requestfailed', (req) => {
 57:         networkLog.push({
 58:           type: 'requestfailed',
 59:           url: req.url(),
 60:           method: req.method(),
 61:           failure: req.failure()?.errorText,
 62:         });
 63:       });
 64: 
 65:       page.on('response', (resp) => {
 66:         if (resp.status() >= 400) {
 67:           networkLog.push({
 68:             type: 'response',
 69:             url: resp.url(),
 70:             status: resp.status(),
 71:           });
 72:         }
 73:       });
 74: 
 75: 
 76:       await page.goto(url.toString(), {
 77:         waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded',
 78:         timeout: TIMEOUT_MS,
 79:       });
 80: 
 81: 
 82:       const consentButtons = [
 83:         'button:has-text("Accept")',
 84:         'button:has-text("I Agree")',
 85:         '[data-testid="accept-button"]',
 86:       ];
 87: 
 88:       for (const sel of consentButtons) {
 89:         const btn = page.locator(sel).first();
 90:         if (await btn.isVisible().catch(() => false)) {
 91:           await btn.click().catch(() => {});
 92:           await page.waitForTimeout(800);
 93:           break;
 94:         }
 95:       }
 96: 
 97: 
 98:       await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle', {
 99:         timeout: 15000,
100:       }).catch(() => {});
101: 
102: 
103:       const anticipatedTab = page.locator('[data-testid="trailer-most-anticipated"]').first();
104:       if (await anticipatedTab.isVisible().catch(() => false)) {
105:         await anticipatedTab.click().catch(() => {});
106:         await page.waitForTimeout(1000);
107:       }
108: 
109: 
110:       const startScroll = Date.now();
111:       while (Date.now() - startScroll < SCROLL_MS) {
112:         await page.mouse.wheel(0, 2000).catch(() => {});
113:         await page.waitForTimeout(400);
114:       }
115: 
116: 
117:       await page.waitForTimeout(2000);
118: 
119:       const html = await page.content();
120:       const screenshot = await page.screenshot({ fullPage: true });
121: 
122:       const safeHost = url.host.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9.-]/g, '_');
123:       const hash = createHash('sha1').update(url.toString()).digest('hex').slice(0, 16);
124: 
125:       const outDir = path.join(process.cwd(), 'temp');
126:       await mkdir(outDir, { recursive: true });
127: 
128:       const htmlName = `${safeHost}-${hash}.rendered.html`;
129:       const shotName = `${safeHost}-${hash}.png`;
130:       const netName = `${safeHost}-${hash}.net.json`;
131: 
132:       await writeFile(path.join(outDir, htmlName), html, 'utf8');
133:       await writeFile(path.join(outDir, shotName), screenshot);
134:       await writeFile(path.join(outDir, netName), JSON.stringify(networkLog, null, 2));
135: 
136:       await context.close();
137: 
138:       return res.status(200).json({
139:         ok: true,
140:         rendered: true,
141:         url: page.url(),
142:         htmlPath: `temp/${htmlName}`,
143:         screenshotPath: `temp/${shotName}`,
144:         networkLogPath: `temp/${netName}`,
145:         size: Buffer.byteLength(html, 'utf8'),
146:         latencyMs: Date.now() - started,
147:       });
148:     } catch (err: any) {
149:       return res.status(500).json({
150:         ok: false,
151:         error: err?.message ?? 'Unknown error',
152:       });
153:     } finally {
154:       await browser.close().catch(() => {});
155:     }
156:   }
157: }
``````

## File: src/asunder/llmBridge.controller.ts
``````typescript
  1: import { All, Controller, Options, Post, Req, Res, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
  2: import type { Request, Response as ExpressResponse } from 'express';
  3: import { randomUUID } from 'node:crypto';
  4: import { BearerTokenGuard } from '../auth/bearer-token.guard';
  5: import { LlmBridgeError, LlmBridgeService } from './llmBridge.service';
  6: 
  7: const JSON_HEADERS = { 'content-type': 'application/json' };
  8: const SSE_HEADERS = {
  9:   'content-type': 'text/event-stream; charset=utf-8',
 10:   connection: 'keep-alive',
 11: };
 12: const CORS_HEADERS = {
 13:   'access-control-allow-origin': '*',
 14:   'access-control-allow-methods': 'POST,OPTIONS',
 15:   'access-control-allow-headers': 'content-type, authorization, x-trace-id',
 16: };
 17: const ALLOW_HEADER = 'POST, OPTIONS';
 18: 
 19: function isMultipart(req: Request): boolean {
 20:   return (req.headers['content-type'] || '').toString().toLowerCase().includes('multipart/form-data');
 21: }
 22: 
 23: function isJson(req: Request): boolean {
 24:   const contentType = (req.headers['content-type'] || '').toString().toLowerCase();
 25:   return contentType === '' || contentType.includes('application/json');
 26: }
 27: 
 28: @Controller('api/asunder')
 29: export class LlmBridgeController {
 30:   constructor(private readonly llmBridgeService: LlmBridgeService) {}
 31: 
 32:   @Options('llmBridge')
 33:   options(@Req() req: Request, @Res() res: ExpressResponse) {
 34:     const traceId = this.resolveTraceId(req);
 35:     res.setHeader('X-Trace-Id', traceId);
 36:     return res.status(204).set({ ...CORS_HEADERS, Allow: ALLOW_HEADER }).send();
 37:   }
 38: 
 39:   @Options('openPrompt')
 40:   openPromptOptions(@Req() req: Request, @Res() res: ExpressResponse) {
 41:     const traceId = this.resolveTraceId(req);
 42:     res.setHeader('X-Trace-Id', traceId);
 43:     return res.status(204).set({ ...CORS_HEADERS, Allow: ALLOW_HEADER }).send();
 44:   }
 45: 
 46:   @Post('llmBridge')
 47:   @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
 48:   async post(@Req() req: Request, @Res() res: ExpressResponse) {
 49:     const traceId = this.resolveTraceId(req);
 50:     const multipart = isMultipart(req);
 51: 
 52:     res.setHeader('X-Trace-Id', traceId);
 53: 
 54:     try {
 55:       if (!isJson(req) && !multipart) {
 56:         throw new LlmBridgeError(415, 'Unsupported media type', {
 57:           error: {
 58:             message: 'Unsupported media type. Use application/json or multipart/form-data',
 59:             type: 'invalid_request_error',
 60:           },
 61:           trace_id: traceId,
 62:         });
 63:       }
 64: 
 65:       const parsed = multipart
 66:         ? await this.llmBridgeService.parseMultipart(req, traceId)
 67:         : { body: req.body, attachments: [] };
 68:       const isStreaming = parsed.body?.stream === true;
 69:       const upstream = await this.llmBridgeService.forward(
 70:         parsed.body,
 71:         traceId,
 72:         parsed.attachments,
 73:       );
 74: 
 75:       if (isStreaming && upstream.ok && upstream.body) {
 76:         return await this.pipeSse(upstream, res, traceId);
 77:       }
 78: 
 79:       return await this.sendJson(upstream, res, traceId);
 80:     } catch (error: any) {
 81:       console.error(
 82:         `[asunder.llmBridge] request failure trace=${traceId} multipart=${multipart}:`,
 83:         error?.stack || error?.message || String(error),
 84:       );
 85:       return this.sendLocalError(res, traceId, error);
 86:     }
 87:   }
 88: 
 89:   @Post('openPrompt')
 90:   @UseGuards(BearerTokenGuard)
 91:   async openPrompt(@Req() req: Request, @Res() res: ExpressResponse) {
 92:     const traceId = this.resolveTraceId(req);
 93:     res.setHeader('X-Trace-Id', traceId);
 94: 
 95:     try {
 96:       if (!isJson(req)) {
 97:         throw new LlmBridgeError(415, 'Unsupported media type', {
 98:           error: {
 99:             message: 'Unsupported media type. Use application/json',
100:             type: 'invalid_request_error',
101:           },
102:           trace_id: traceId,
103:         });
104:       }
105: 
106:       const body = this.normalizeOpenPromptBody(req.body);
107:       const upstream = await this.llmBridgeService.forward(body, traceId);
108: 
109:       return await this.sendJson(upstream, res, traceId);
110:     } catch (error: any) {
111:       console.error(
112:         `[asunder.openPrompt] request failure trace=${traceId}:`,
113:         error?.stack || error?.message || String(error),
114:       );
115:       return this.sendLocalError(res, traceId, error);
116:     }
117:   }
118: 
119:   @All('llmBridge')
120:   methodNotAllowed(@Req() req: Request, @Res() res: ExpressResponse) {
121:     const traceId = this.resolveTraceId(req);
122:     const body = {
123:       errors: ['Method not allowed'],
124:       trace_id: traceId,
125:     };
126: 
127:     res.setHeader('X-Trace-Id', traceId);
128:     return res
129:       .status(405)
130:       .set({ ...JSON_HEADERS, ...CORS_HEADERS, Allow: ALLOW_HEADER })
131:       .send(JSON.stringify(body));
132:   }
133: 
134:   @All('openPrompt')
135:   openPromptMethodNotAllowed(@Req() req: Request, @Res() res: ExpressResponse) {
136:     const traceId = this.resolveTraceId(req);
137:     const body = {
138:       errors: ['Method not allowed'],
139:       trace_id: traceId,
140:     };
141: 
142:     res.setHeader('X-Trace-Id', traceId);
143:     return res
144:       .status(405)
145:       .set({ ...JSON_HEADERS, ...CORS_HEADERS, Allow: ALLOW_HEADER })
146:       .send(JSON.stringify(body));
147:   }
148: 
149:   private async pipeSse(
150:     upstream: globalThis.Response,
151:     res: ExpressResponse,
152:     traceId: string,
153:   ) {
154:     res.status(upstream.status);
155:     res.set({
156:       ...CORS_HEADERS,
157:       ...SSE_HEADERS,
158:       'Cache-Control': 'no-store',
159:     });
160:     res.flushHeaders();
161: 
162:     const reader = upstream.body?.getReader();
163:     if (!reader) {
164:       res.end();
165:       return;
166:     }
167: 
168:     try {
169:       while (true) {
170:         const { value, done } = await reader.read();
171:         if (done) break;
172:         if (value) res.write(Buffer.from(value));
173:       }
174:     } catch (error: any) {
175:       console.error(
176:         `[asunder.llmBridge] stream error trace=${traceId}:`,
177:         error?.message ?? String(error),
178:       );
179:     } finally {
180:       res.end();
181:     }
182:   }
183: 
184:   private async sendJson(
185:     upstream: globalThis.Response,
186:     res: ExpressResponse,
187:     traceId: string,
188:   ) {
189:     const text = await upstream.text().catch(() => '');
190:     const contentType = upstream.headers.get('content-type') || JSON_HEADERS['content-type'];
191:     const xRequestId = upstream.headers.get('x-request-id');
192:     const headers: Record<string, string> = {
193:       ...CORS_HEADERS,
194:       'Cache-Control': 'no-store',
195:       'content-type': contentType,
196:     };
197: 
198:     if (xRequestId) headers['x-request-id'] = xRequestId;
199: 
200:     res.setHeader('X-Trace-Id', traceId);
201:     return res.status(upstream.status).set(headers).send(text);
202:   }
203: 
204:   private sendLocalError(res: ExpressResponse, traceId: string, error: unknown) {
205:     if (error instanceof LlmBridgeError) {
206:       const body =
207:         error.body && typeof error.body === 'object'
208:           ? { ...(error.body as Record<string, unknown>), trace_id: traceId }
209:           : {
210:               error: {
211:                 message: error.message,
212:                 type: 'server_error',
213:               },
214:               trace_id: traceId,
215:             };
216: 
217:       return res
218:         .status(error.statusCode)
219:         .set({ ...JSON_HEADERS, ...CORS_HEADERS, 'Cache-Control': 'no-store' })
220:         .send(JSON.stringify(body));
221:     }
222: 
223:     const details =
224:       error instanceof Error ? error.message : typeof error === 'string' ? error : 'Unexpected failure';
225:     const body = {
226:       error: {
227:         message: 'Internal server error',
228:         type: 'server_error',
229:         details,
230:       },
231:       trace_id: traceId,
232:     };
233: 
234:     return res
235:       .status(500)
236:       .set({ ...JSON_HEADERS, ...CORS_HEADERS, 'Cache-Control': 'no-store' })
237:       .send(JSON.stringify(body));
238:   }
239: 
240:   private normalizeOpenPromptBody(value: unknown) {
241:     if (!value || typeof value !== 'object' || Array.isArray(value)) {
242:       throw new LlmBridgeError(400, 'Request body must be a JSON object', {
243:         error: {
244:           message: 'Request body must be a JSON object',
245:           type: 'invalid_request_error',
246:         },
247:       });
248:     }
249: 
250:     const body = value as Record<string, unknown>;
251:     const promptId = typeof body.promptId === 'string' ? body.promptId.trim() : '';
252:     const promptVersionValue = body.promptVersion ?? body.prompytVersion;
253:     const input = typeof body.input === 'string' ? body.input : undefined;
254:     const stream = body.stream ?? false;
255:     const model = (process.env.OPENAI_ACTIVE_MODEL || '').trim();
256: 
257:     if (!model) {
258:       throw new LlmBridgeError(500, 'OPENAI_ACTIVE_MODEL missing', {
259:         error: {
260:           message: 'Server misconfigured: OPENAI_ACTIVE_MODEL missing',
261:           type: 'server_error',
262:         },
263:       });
264:     }
265: 
266:     if (!promptId) {
267:       throw new LlmBridgeError(400, 'promptId must be a non-empty string', {
268:         error: {
269:           message: 'promptId must be a non-empty string',
270:           type: 'invalid_request_error',
271:         },
272:       });
273:     }
274: 
275:     if (!Number.isInteger(promptVersionValue) || Number(promptVersionValue) < 1) {
276:       throw new LlmBridgeError(400, 'promptVersion must be a positive integer', {
277:         error: {
278:           message: 'promptVersion must be a positive integer',
279:           type: 'invalid_request_error',
280:         },
281:       });
282:     }
283: 
284:     if (typeof input !== 'string' || !input.trim()) {
285:       throw new LlmBridgeError(400, 'input must be a non-empty string', {
286:         error: {
287:           message: 'input must be a non-empty string',
288:           type: 'invalid_request_error',
289:         },
290:       });
291:     }
292: 
293:     if (typeof stream !== 'boolean') {
294:       throw new LlmBridgeError(400, 'stream must be a boolean', {
295:         error: {
296:           message: 'stream must be a boolean',
297:           type: 'invalid_request_error',
298:         },
299:       });
300:     }
301: 
302:     return {
303:       model,
304:       promptId,
305:       promptVersion: Number(promptVersionValue),
306:       input,
307:       stream,
308:     };
309:   }
310: 
311:   private resolveTraceId(req: Request): string {
312:     return (req.header('x-trace-id') || req.header('X-Trace-Id') || '').trim() || randomUUID();
313:   }
314: }
``````

## File: src/main.ts
``````typescript
 1: import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
 2: import { AppModule } from './app.module';
 3: import { DocumentBuilder, SwaggerModule } from '@nestjs/swagger';
 4: 
 5: import * as express from 'express';
 6: 
 7: async function bootstrap() {
 8:   const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule);
 9: 
10:   app.use(express.json({ limit: '30mb' }));
11:   app.use(express.urlencoded({ limit: '30mb', extended: true }));
12: 
13:   const env = process.env.NODE_ENV ?? 'development';
14:   const enableSwagger = process.env.ENABLE_SWAGGER === 'true' || env !== 'production';
15: 
16:   if (enableSwagger) {
17:     const config = new DocumentBuilder()
18:       .setTitle('API')
19:       .setDescription('NestJS API')
20:       .setVersion(process.env.npm_package_version ?? '0.0.0')
21:       .build();
22: 
23:     const document = SwaggerModule.createDocument(app, config);
24:     SwaggerModule.setup('docs', app, document);
25:   }
26: 
27:   const port = parseInt(process.env.PORT ?? '3000', 10);
28:   await app.listen(port);
29: }
30: bootstrap();
``````

## File: Makefile
``````makefile
 1: ifneq (,$(wildcard .env))
 2: include .env
 3: export
 4: endif
 5: 
 6: PKG_NAME := $(shell npm pkg get name | tr -d '"')
 7: PKG_VERSION := $(shell npm pkg get version | tr -d '"')
 8: RN_VERSION := $(shell npm pkg get dependencies.react-native | tr -d '"')
 9: 
10: serve:
11: 	pnpm start:dev
12: 
13: bump:
14: 	pnpm run bump
15: 
16: repomix:
17: 	@mkdir -p temp
18: 	@npx repomix@latest --style markdown -o temp/repomix-$(PKG_NAME)-$(PKG_VERSION).md
19: # smaller + more “structural” (less raw noise)
20: # npx repomix@latest --compress --parsable-style
21: 
22: git-push:
23: 	@npm run bump
24: 	@git add .
25: 	@git commit -m "$(m)"
26: 	@git push origin $(shell git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)
27: 
28: git-file:
29: 	@git checkout -- $(m)
30: 
31: git-set:
32: 	git remote set-url origin git@github.com-kiaspora:kiaspora/kia-mobile-app.git;ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_kiaspora
``````
